data.text: I’m checking the repo state and the bead’s prerequisites first, because this benchmark bead may be blocked by the backend work it depends on. If the needed backend and interface are not present, I’ll verify that and then either implement the benchmark or write the required no-changes rationale with a concrete blocker.

data.output:
# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion

- **Epic beads** are aggregates by design. Criterion (c) (specific test names) and (d) (wired-in) commonly pass via children. The epic should still satisfy (a), (b), (e) — pointing at the children's collective `go test` set — and (f), (g), (h).
- **Doc-only beads** legitimately omit (c) (no tests) and (d) (no code path). They must still satisfy (e)'s lefthook clause and (h).
- **Pure deletion / rename beads** legitimately omit (d). They must still cite the deletion-target's file:line in (b) and assert byte-identical behavior preservation in (c).

---

## 7. References

- `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md` (planned via `ddx-06b77652`) — P7 source.
- `.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md` — the audit that produced this template.
- `.agents/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` and `.claude/skills/ddx/reference/beads.md` — operational guidance on creating beads via `ddx bead create`.

ID:       ddx-781a15cf
Title:    Benchmark: axon backend vs JSONL on 1100-bead archive
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 2
Labels:   phase:2,  area:beads,  area:storage,  kind:perf,  backend-migration, triage:needs-investigation
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-5d49b14e
Deps:     ddx-c9ef6c4f
Desc:     PROBLEM
No performance benchmark exists comparing axon backend vs JSONL backend at realistic archive scale (1100 beads). Without this benchmark, the parent epic (ddx-5d49b14e) cannot evaluate its AC6 gate ('axon parity-or-better than JSONL for ready/blocked/show queue ops').

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/bead/ contains backend_jsonl.go but no axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5, a dep, must land first).
- No benchmark file exercises ready/blocked/show operations on a 1100-bead corpus.
- The missing dep edge (ddx-781a15cf → ddx-95ec5ed5) caused the queue to surface this bead prematurely (dep edge should be added via ).

PROPOSED FIX
- After ddx-95ec5ed5 (axon backend) and ddx-bbdd7564 (Backend interface) land:
  - Add cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go (or bead_bench_test.go) with:
    - BenchmarkReady_JSONL_1100: ready queue ops on 1100-bead JSONL corpus.
    - BenchmarkReady_Axon_1100: same ops on 1100-bead axon corpus.
    - BenchmarkBlocked_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkBlocked_Axon_1100.
    - BenchmarkShow_JSONL_1100 and BenchmarkShow_Axon_1100.
  - Generate 1100-bead test corpus (mix of open + closed + archived).
  - Capture wall-time and allocs. Commit results to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/axon-vs-jsonl-benchmark.md.

NON-SCOPE
- axon backend implementation (ddx-95ec5ed5).
- Adding a CI gate on benchmark results (gate belongs to a follow-up bead).
Accept:   1. Benchmark file exists at cli/internal/bead/benchmark_backends_test.go covering BenchmarkReady, BenchmarkBlocked, BenchmarkShow for both jsonl and axon on 1100-bead corpus.
2. Runs via: cd cli && go test -bench=. ./internal/bead/... -run='^$' green.
3. Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl (wall-time + allocs) committed to .ddx/executions/<run-id>/axon-vs-jsonl-benchmark.md.
4. Benchmark is reproducible via go test -bench.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... green (unit tests unaffected).
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

reason: The axon bead backend is not yet implemented, so the benchmark this bead asks for cannot be written end-to-end. AC2 ("Runs against both jsonl and axon backends") and AC3 ("Results document parity-or-better for axon vs jsonl") require an axon Backend implementation; none exists in cli/internal/bead/.

Evidence checked:
- `cli/internal/bead/axon/` does not exist (Glob).
- No `BackendAxon` constant, no `axon.New*`, no `NewAxonBackend` references in cli/internal/bead (Grep).
- `cli/internal/bead/backend.go` exposes only `BackendJSONL`, `BackendBD`, `BackendBR`.
- TD-030 (docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md) is the design spec; it lists the bench (test plan §4) as a Beta gate.
- Sibling beads under parent ddx-5d49b14e show the prerequisite work is still open:
  * ddx-8d747049 open — "axon backend: prototype implementation via genqlient-generated GraphQL Go client"
  * ddx-95ec5ed5 open — "Implement axon Backend for bead tracker"
  * ddx-743bc194 open — "Run chaos_test.go against axon backend"
  * ddx-6602559c open — "Bench: queue ops parity (axon vs JSONL) on 1100-bead archive" (duplicate of this bead)

Follow-up: this bead should depend on the axon backend implementation beads (ddx-8d747049 and/or ddx-95ec5ed5) and become ready only once those close. Alternatively, dedupe with ddx-6602559c. A jsonl-only benchmark on the 1100-bead corpus could be filed as a separate bead if a baseline is wanted before axon lands, but the AC as written here cannot be satisfied without the axon backend.

New cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go defines ReviewVerdict struct with Verdict (string, enum-validated), Findings ([]Finding), Summary (string), and SchemaVersion (int) fields.\n2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.\n6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events_attachment":"ddx-021bd69b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:39:11.049944919Z","id":"ddx-021bd69b","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","kind:implementation","area:agent","area:review"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b2bae304","status":"closed","title":"review: machine-readable JSON verdict contract + replace markdown extractor","updated_at":"2026-04-25T09:40:01.996055932Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:61:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events_attachment":"ddx-0a33bc5f/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent: provider error: provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T13:19:44Z","feature":"FEAT-019","id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-019","efficacy","perf"],"notes":"Efficacy now depends on ddx-2ceb02fa. The current worktree contains the sharded sessions index implementation at .ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl, so this pass reads that substrate directly; no interim source or stub was used.\n\nSource-change baseline before per-shard memoization: fixture seeds 10,000 session index rows across 2 monthly shards with 10 distinct harness/provider/model groups, plus stretch fixture of 50,000 rows across 24 monthly shards. Measured with go test ./internal/server/graphql -run TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets -count=1 -v.\n\nSource-only results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 46.87735ms (target \u003c=150ms)\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 47.756933ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 24.356549ms (target \u003c=60ms)\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 25.628964ms (target \u003c=200ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 279.266233ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 300.428036ms (target \u003c=1000ms)\n\nFinal implementation adds shard-stat invalidated session-index memoization. Final measured results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 1.368873ms\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 3.202329ms\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 1.742706ms\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 2.049998ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 242.886602ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 886.748µs\n\nDB-decision protocol: in-place session-index aggregation met the first-pass and stretch targets, so no axon/sqlite/postgres substrate proposal is required in this pass. Race-detector runs skip the perf-threshold test because race instrumentation invalidates latency targets; non-race CI enforces them.\n\nHardware/footer: Linux eitri 6.17.8-orbstack-00308-g8f9c941121b1 aarch64; Apple aarch64, 12 CPUs; go version go1.26.2 linux/arm64.\n\n`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:233` — The Playwright smoke test does open `/efficacy`, renders rows, and clicks into attempt details, but it uses `mockEfficacy(page, { rows: TEN_K_SESSION_FIXTURE_ROWS })` at line 234. That is only 12 pre-aggregated mocked rows, not a seeded 10k session-index fixture, so it does not validate page behavior or navigation budget against the real backend fixture required by AC8.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**No resolver changes.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is untouched. AC1 requires `EfficacyRows` to read from the sharded sessions index and remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`.\n**No fixture or benchmarks.** AC3/AC4 require a ≥10k-row fixture across 2+ shards with baseline latency measurements. Nothing seeds data or measures performance.\n**No tests of any kind.** AC6 (completeness/superset), AC7 (freshness), and AC8 (Playwright smoke) all require new test files. None are present.\n**No schema changes.** AC2 requires optional `since`, `until`, `projectId` filter args on the GraphQL query. No `.graphql` or resolver signature changes appear.\n**The result.json itself reports `task_succeeded`**, but the merge commit (`41fa9657`) it references only contains \"test(efficacy): add real-backend HTTP smoke + align UI fixture\" per git log — suggesting partial work may exist on a branch that wasn't included in this diff. The next agent should check whether commit `41fa9657` contains unreflected work, or whether the execution truly produced no implementation.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is not changed; `EfficacyRows` is not shown reading `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, and the old `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo` removals are absent.\nGraphQL schema/resolver argument changes for nullable `since`, `until`, and `projectId` are absent from the diff.\nNo fixture or benchmark changes seed 10k+ session rows across 2+ shards or record baseline/perf results in bead notes.\nNo tests are added or changed for non-bead sessions, freshness after session index writes, or old-path superset comparison.\nNo Playwright smoke test for `/efficacy` is added or changed.\nNo tracker or bead-note evidence records dependency on `ddx-2ceb02fa` or a DB-decision proposal.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-282fb10f","status":"closed","title":"Efficacy page is slow and sources from the wrong place: rework as session aggregation, seed ≥1k fixture, raise DB decision if in-place insufficient","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:48.776241989Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:66:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.\n\n## Observed\n\nFrom CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:\n\n  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json\n  {\n    \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",  # WRONG\n    \"state\": \"running\",\n    \"worker_id\": \"worker-20260415T011331-d0a4\"\n  }\n\nI wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.\n\nCurrently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.\n\n## Scope\n\n- --project \u003cpath\u003e or --project \u003cname\u003e flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use\n- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar\n- Default to CWD if unset and no flag\n- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error\n- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root\n\n## Why this matters for the user's workflow\n\nWithout this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run\n- [ ] Env var fallback documented\n- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection\n- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission\n- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events_attachment":"ddx-0b9c980a/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:71:{"acceptance":"ddx agent log --bead axon-abc123 shows all execution attempts for that bead in chronological order with ATTEMPT, STARTED, DURATION, HARNESS, MODEL, OUTCOME, TOKENS, COST, SESSION columns. OUTCOME is resolved from result.json when available, falls back to exit_code. Summary line printed after table. --json emits filtered SessionEntry array with outcome field. Bead with no sessions prints 'no sessions found for bead \u003cid\u003e'. Existing ddx agent log behavior (no --bead flag) is unchanged. ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e single-session detail view is unchanged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T10:13:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:35:04.508596568Z","description":"ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.\n\nThis bead adds --bead \u003cid\u003e filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nWhen --bead \u003cbead-id\u003e is provided:\n  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl\n  - Filter to entries where Correlation[\"bead_id\"] == bead-id\n  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)\n  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)\n\nNo change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).\n\n### 2. Attempt history table columns\nATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION\n- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order\n- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)\n- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e\n- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0\n\n### 3. Outcome resolution\nSessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:\n  - Use Correlation[\"attempt_id\"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json\n  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file\n  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent\n\n### 4. Summary line\nAfter the table, print a one-line summary:\n  '\u003cbead-id\u003e: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: \u003celapsed\u003e ago.'\n\n### 5. --json passthrough\nWhen --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention\n- .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl — runtime data source","events_attachment":"ddx-0c40e959/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T10:25:22.090512411Z","id":"ddx-0c40e959","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","observability","cli","agent-log"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-4d938dd","status":"closed","title":"Extend ddx agent log with --bead filter and per-bead attempt history","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:42.487751389Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:101:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer running `go test`, my test runs do not leave behind registered projects in my real `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`. When a machine has already been polluted, I can prune it with one command. The server sweep is aggressive enough that obvious test-dir pollution does not survive a restart.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Fix A — test isolation enforced by CI.**\n   - Every existing test that instantiates `ServerState` or boots a server helper is migrated to an isolated state dir. No test writes to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json`.\n   - A lint test (Go or shell) parses `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` (and any other directories that wire up `ServerState`) and fails if it finds a construction that does not pass an explicit isolated state path or override `XDG_DATA_HOME` first. Test cases added for both the allowed-pattern and the disallowed-pattern.\n\n2. **Fix B — sweep recognizes test-dir pollution.**\n   - `ServerState.migrate` drops entries whose `Path` matches any of:\n     - prefix `/tmp/`, `/private/tmp/`, `/var/folders/`\n     - regex `/Test[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*\\d+/` anywhere in the path\n   - Missing paths matching these patterns are dropped on first sweep (no 24h tombstone).\n   - Reachable paths matching these patterns are still dropped (they are never real projects — the test dir may exist transiently but is never the user's registered project).\n   - Unit test seeds a fixture state with one real project, one `/tmp/TestFoo123/001` missing, one `/tmp/TestBar456/002` still on disk, and one `/private/tmp/TestMac789/001`; asserts only the real project survives.\n\n3. **Fix C — pruning CLI.**\n   - `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` applies the Fix B rules to an on-disk state file.\n   - Writes `server-state.json.bak-\u003cYYYYMMDDTHHMMSS\u003e` before overwriting.\n   - Prints summary: `Pruned X of Y entries (Z kept)`. Dry-run prints the summary without writing.\n   - Integration test uses a fixture state containing 10 real + 100 fake entries, runs the command, asserts 10 remain and a backup file with exactly 110 entries was written.\n\n4. **Perf amplifier — regression test.**\n   - A test asserts `GetBeadSnapshots` does **not** call `os.Stat` on any project already tombstoned or pattern-filtered. (Implementation detail: sweep on startup + skip tombstoned in the hot loop.)\n   - If Fix A+B are in place, a fresh `go test ./...` run followed by a server restart produces a state file with ≤ 20 projects on a typical dev machine. Asserted via a CI job that runs the tests, starts the server briefly, inspects the state file, and fails if test-dir patterns are present.\n\n5. **Backward compatibility.**\n   - The prune CLI is safe to run on an existing polluted state file. The sweep upgrade is safe on restart — no data loss for real projects.\n   - On upgrade, a note is logged: \"Pruned X phantom test-dir projects from state file.\"\n\n6. **Cross-reference.**\n   - Bead notes link to `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) as the two surfaces whose perf problems are amplified by this bug. Those beads' fixture sizing assumptions were correct for a clean state file; the operator experience will still feel slow on a polluted machine until Fix C is run.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"a101e215a0b34590951f51340d2f25c07ff1c5cc","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:44:13.806406362Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json` on a developer machine contains **7,343 registered projects**. Composition:\n\n- **6,936 named \"001\"** — all are Go test temp dirs like `/tmp/TestAgentCapabilitiesCommandJSON1451829170/001`, `/tmp/TestAgentCheckSuccess3666443068/001`.\n- **344 named \"002\"** — same pattern in tests that use `002/`.\n- **~60 real projects** (ddx, helix, axon, plus per-branch worktrees).\n- Only 135 of the 6,936 are marked unreachable; the rest are \"alive\" because their `/tmp` dir still exists or because `LastSeen` is recent.\n- State file size: 1.8 MB.\n\n## Root cause\n\n1. Go tests that exercise the server (or anything that wires up a `ServerState`) call `RegisterProject` with a temp-dir path. The state file they write to is **not isolated** — it defaults to the shared XDG location (`serverAddrDir()` at `cli/internal/server/server.go:67`) unless the test explicitly overrides `XDG_DATA_HOME` or constructs state via the `tc.StateDir` injection path.\n2. There is no test-side deregistration on teardown. Temp dirs get cleaned up by `t.TempDir()`'s own finalizer, but the project entry in the production state file stays.\n3. The reachability sweep (`ServerState.migrate` at `cli/internal/server/state.go:85-148`) tombstones missing-path entries and drops them 24h later. With thousands of entries being added per test run, the rate of addition outpaces the 24h GC.\n\n## Perf amplifier (cross-cutting)\n\nBoth `GetBeadSnapshots` (`state_graphql.go:49-105`) and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-489`) iterate every registered project per request. With N=7343 that's ~7k `os.Stat` + `store.ReadAll` attempts per bead-list or sessions query, mostly against dead paths. This is part of why `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed) feel slow in practice even before their own N·M bugs are considered.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nThree independently-landable fixes:\n\n### Fix A — Test isolation (highest leverage)\n\n- Audit every test that calls `ServerState` / `RegisterProject` / boots a server. Each one must point at an isolated state dir (`t.TempDir()` + `XDG_DATA_HOME` override, or direct `tc.StateDir` injection).\n- Add a linting test: parse `cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` for constructions of `ServerState` or HTTP server test helpers and assert each uses an isolated state dir. Fail CI otherwise.\n- This is the real fix. The rest is damage mitigation.\n\n### Fix B — Smarter sweep (defense in depth)\n\n- Sweep: any path under `/tmp`, `/private/tmp`, `/var/folders/*/T/` (macOS test temp root), or matching `/tmp/Test*` should be treated as a **candidate test-dir project** and dropped immediately on first sweep that finds it missing — no 24h tombstone.\n- Sweep should also drop entries where `Path` contains any `Test[A-Z]\\w+\\d+` segment (the Go testing convention), regardless of whether the dir currently exists. These are never real projects.\n- Unit test: seed a state with mixed real + fake-test-dir entries, run sweep, assert only the real ones remain.\n\n### Fix C — Manual cleanup CLI\n\n- `ddx server state prune` — read state file, apply Fix B's rules, write backup to `~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json.bak-\u003ctimestamp\u003e`, write cleaned state, print summary (X dropped, Y kept).\n- Useful for recovery on machines that have already accumulated pollution — like the reporter's. Keep even after Fix A lands.\n- Dry-run flag prints the summary without writing.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Moving state to SQLite.\n- Per-user project management UI. (Follow-up if the projects list becomes a real operator surface.)\n- Renaming \"001\" away from a confusing default. The name field is just `filepath.Base(path)`; fixing Fix A makes this question moot.","events_attachment":"ddx-15f7ee0b/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-020","foo":"bar","id":"ddx-15f7ee0b","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-020","feat-008","testing","hygiene","perf-amplifier"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1 (Fix A):** No changes to any `*_test.go` files to isolate `ServerState` construction, and no lint test added.\n**AC 2 (Fix B):** No changes to `cli/internal/server/state.go` (`ServerState.migrate`) to add test-dir pattern matching or immediate-drop logic. No unit test with seeded fixture state.\n**AC 3 (Fix C):** No `ddx server state prune` command added in `cli/cmd/`. No backup/dry-run logic. No integration test.\n**AC 4 (Perf):** No regression test asserting `GetBeadSnapshots` skips tombstoned/filtered projects.\n**AC 5 (Compat):** No upgrade log message implemented.\n**AC 6 (Cross-ref):** No bead notes linking to `ddx-9ce6842a` or `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\nThe `result.json` reports `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merged commit `af88d432` (referenced as `result_rev`) had message \"fix(server): drop phantom test-dir projects from state file\" — the actual implementation may have been merged already but is not visible in this diff. The review diff only covers `034fbf8f`, which is the evidence commit, not the implementation commit.\n\nCross-reference: This state-file pollution amplifies `ddx-9ce6842a` (beads perf) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions feed). Their fixture sizing assumptions are correct for a clean state file; operator experience on already-polluted machines remains slow until `ddx server state prune` is run.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/server/**/*_test.go` — no test isolation migration or CI lint test is present in the diff.\n`cli/internal/server/state.go`, `ServerState.migrate` — no test-dir pattern filtering, immediate-drop behavior, or phantom-project pruning log is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_test.go` or equivalent — no sweep fixture test for real, missing `/tmp/Test...`, reachable `/tmp/Test...`, and `/private/tmp/Test...` projects is present.\n`cli/cmd/` or server command wiring — no `ddx server state prune [--dry-run] [--state \u003cpath\u003e]` implementation is present.\nCLI integration tests — no prune fixture test asserting 10 kept entries and a 110-entry backup is present.\n`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`, `GetBeadSnapshots` tests, or CI workflow files — no regression test or CI assertion for skipping tombstoned/pattern-filtered projects is present.\nBead metadata/notes — no cross-reference notes linking `ddx-9ce6842a` and `ddx-2ceb02fa` are present in the reviewed diff.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-9b85f519","status":"closed","title":"Test runs pollute ~/.local/share/ddx/server-state.json with thousands of /tmp/Test.../001 projects","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:07:09.859726773Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:137:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T01:13:31Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604151773f9b905346ee95b051e024a02f850cf0","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:11:17.497753676Z","description":"The embedded 'agent' harness can complete a task successfully (commit produced, tests pass) but then fail to terminate, getting stuck in a compaction retry loop with compaction always failing. The parent execute-loop never observes task_succeeded and hangs forever.\n\n## Observed\n\nRunning 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --local --harness agent --model qwen/qwen3-coder-next' against axon-43ed769b (axon auth-core-types bead):\n\n1. qwen3-coder-next ran the task correctly, producing commit 91d4bdb with 636 LOC of types + 109 green tests + clippy clean. The commit was present in the worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-axon-43ed769b-20260415T005652-ebe7a16a.\n\n2. Instead of terminating, the agent kept generating events. After ~20 minutes the session log was 33 MB (8461+ events, 249 messages) and stuck in:\n\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.start\",...}\n    {\"type\":\"compaction.end\",...,\"data\":{\"messages_after\":249,\"messages_before\":249,\"no_compaction\":true,\"success\":false}}\n    ...\n\n3. Parent ddx process in __futex_wait. Had to SIGTERM the parent (PID 4141811) and manually fast-forward-merge the worktree commit to land the work.\n\n4. A1 (previous bead) ran the same harness/model and terminated correctly in ~14 min with identical-looking commit shape. The difference was bead size: A2's output was a single 633-line Rust file, vs A1's 350-line SQL + 290-line test file. A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.\n\n## Expected\n\n- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate\n- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after \u003c messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely\n- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin\n\n## Scope\n\n- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status\n- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn\n- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls\n- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget\n\n## Evidence\n\n- Session log: /Users/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/executions/20260415T005652-ebe7a16a/embedded/agent-1776214612561376672.jsonl (33 MB)\n- Rescued commit: 91d4bdb on axon master\n- Parent process: PID 4141811 (killed)\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] Compaction retry loop is bounded and fails structurally\n- [ ] Post-commit termination path is deterministic\n- [ ] Watchdog detects and terminates stuck sessions with a preserved-commit outcome\n- [ ] Regression test reproduces the stuck state (seeded session with large no-op tool calls)","events_attachment":"ddx-25a41f51/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T01:39:31.55993889Z","id":"ddx-25a41f51","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:stability"],"owner":"ddx","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-e80e780a","status":"closed","title":"bug: embedded agent stalls in post-commit compaction loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:46:00.206978601Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:145:{"acceptance":"- [ ] Option A, B, or C selected and documented in an ADR or design note under the ddx repo\n- [ ] Implementation lands with migration path for existing .ddx/beads.jsonl files (no lost bead history)\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ workers on the same machine produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Concurrent writes from 2+ machines (via ddx server or shared remote) produce a deterministic, semantically correct tracker state\n- [ ] Claim races are resolved by exactly one winner; losers see a structured 'already_claimed' error\n- [ ] Crash recovery: a claimed bead whose worker died returns to unclaimed after the heartbeat timeout (existing mechanism continues to work)\n- [ ] Existing beads.jsonl format continues to work as a read-only snapshot for offline tooling (jq queries, etc.)\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests covering the concurrency scenarios above\n- [ ] Migration test: apply the upgrade to axon's current beads.jsonl (748+ beads on sindri side, 30+ new on eitri side) and verify no semantic loss","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:46.447948869Z","description":"Fourth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger. Multiple writers on different machines or in parallel worker processes produce divergent files that require manual textual merge. git merge can sometimes append-merge JSONL cleanly if each side touches disjoint lines, but when both sides touch the same bead (claim / close / add-event), the merge is semantically broken even when git reports it as resolved.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, eitri and sindri both wrote to beads.jsonl independently for 14 days. Local has 134 commits touching the tracker; origin has 336. When I tried to rebase my planning commit onto origin, git reported conflicts in .ddx/beads.jsonl — and these conflicts would have been 'resolved' by git's textual merge logic even when the semantic outcome is wrong (e.g. two machines claiming the same bead with different claimed-machine values).\n\nThe existing execute-loop help text says:\n\n    By default execute-loop submits to the running ddx server as a background\n    worker and returns immediately. Use --local to run inline in the current\n    process.\n\nSo a ddx server EXISTS and is supposed to coordinate. But .ddx/beads.jsonl is still the ledger, which means the server is not the source of truth — it's just a submission surface. The git-tracked file is authoritative, and that's what's racing.\n\n## Options (pick one during implementation)\n\n**A. Server-authoritative tracker.** When the ddx server is running, it owns beads.jsonl. All reads and writes go through the server's API. The git-tracked file becomes a periodic snapshot for backup/offline use, not the source of truth. Workers on multiple machines talk to a shared server.\n\n**B. CRDT append-only log.** Replace the current representation with an append-only log keyed by (machine_id, bead_id, sequence, timestamp). Each writer appends to its own partition; readers compute the effective state by merging partitions with a conflict-resolution rule (last-writer-wins per (bead_id, field), or better). git textual merge of the partitioned log is always safe because writers never share lines.\n\n**C. Per-bead lock files with atomic rename.** Each bead mutation acquires a lock file (beads/\u003cid\u003e.lock), writes beads/\u003cid\u003e.json atomically via rename, releases the lock. The JSONL aggregate is regenerated from per-bead files. Git tracks per-bead files, not the aggregate. Safe across local parallelism but not across machines without network coordination.\n\n**Recommendation**: A is the cleanest if the ddx server is already deployed as authoritative infrastructure. B is the best fit for the current decentralized model. C is a stopgap that solves intra-machine parallelism but not multi-machine drift.\n\n## Scope note\n\nDo NOT conflate this bead with the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57). Those three fix the git-level divergence; this bead fixes the tracker-level divergence. Both are needed — the triad without tracker safety would still produce semantically-conflicting beads.jsonl merges even when the underlying git history is linear.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Two workers on the same machine claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly\n- Two workers on different machines claim different beads concurrently: both claims persist correctly (requires network coordination)\n- Two workers attempt to claim the same bead: one wins, the other sees 'already claimed' and picks a different one\n- Recovery from crash between claim and CloseWithEvidence: the bead returns to unclaimed after a heartbeat timeout\n- Tracker state is deterministic from any valid ordering of concurrent writes (CRDT option)","id":"ddx-2791dc4f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","tracker","concurrency"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-820c201","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: .ddx/beads.jsonl concurrent-write safety","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:45:05.880884507Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:160:{"acceptance":"AC1. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-project via .ddx/config.yaml under agent.routing or agent.endpoints — e.g. agent.endpoints[provider].request_timeout_seconds — and the current 15-minute hardcoded constant becomes a default that can be overridden upward (or removed entirely) per provider.\n\nAC2. ProviderTimeout is configurable per-tier via the model-overrides path: cheap tier on a known thinking model (qwen3.6, deepseek-r1, etc.) defaults to a longer cap (e.g. 60 min or unlimited). Cloud chat models keep the 15-min default.\n\nAC3. The idle-read timeout (DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 min) is the primary stalled-TCP-socket defense; documentation in cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go and the user-facing config docs explain the relationship between idle-read and wall-clock-request timeouts and when each fires.\n\nAC4. When ProviderTimeout fires, the error message names the exact timeout setting and the path to override it (e.g. 'request_timeout=15m exceeded; configure agent.endpoints.\u003cname\u003e.request_timeout_seconds in .ddx/config.yaml').\n\nAC5. execute-loop / execute-bead --request-timeout DURATION CLI flag exists for one-off overrides during debugging.\n\nAC6. Default for known-thinking-model surfaces (provider.type=lmstudio AND model name matches qwen3.6/qwen-r1/deepseek-r1 etc., or some other declared 'thinking' attribute) is bumped to a sensible value (e.g. 60 min) without requiring per-project config.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T20:58:18Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"99375eaf330ea13838d40d2176d193ae3cd3453b","created_at":"2026-04-29T18:50:46.475563514Z","description":"Source: cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:30\n  const DefaultProviderRequestTimeout = 15 * time.Minute\n\nThis is hardcoded, not surfaced through .ddx/config.yaml or any flag, and is applied uniformly to every provider regardless of model class. It is set on every service path:\n  - cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:116  ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n  - cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:153           ProviderTimeout: DefaultProviderRequestTimeout\n\nThe comment justifies it as 'Defeats RC4 of ddx-0a651925: a stalled TCP socket that has delivered headers but stopped emitting body bytes would otherwise pin a goroutine until the outer wall-clock (3h) frees it.'\n\nBut that scenario is already handled by a separate idle-read timeout immediately below, on line 34:\n  const DefaultProviderIdleReadTimeout = 5 * time.Minute\n\nThe 5-minute idle-read timeout is exactly the right tool for the stalled-TCP-socket case (no body bytes for 5 min → fail). The 15-minute wall-clock cap on a single request is the WRONG tool — it kills any legitimately long single Chat call regardless of streaming health.\n\nConcrete impact observed today (2026-04-29 axon repo):\n\n1. axon-db7a6d0a worker (qwen3.6-35b-a3b on bragi via lmstudio)\n   - status: no_changes\n   - duration_ms: 1,762,081 (29 min total — one timeout + retry)\n   - log: 'WARN provider error, retrying attempt=1 err=\"provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s\" delay=1s'\n   - Retry consumed another full 15 min, also timed out, no commits produced.\n\n2. axon-80979cb8 + axon-db7a6d0a + axon-ab2e52e0 (3-worker cascade re-launch)\n   - All 3 hit 'provider request timeout: wall-clock 15m0s' on the first attempt\n   - User killed the run before retries could waste another 15 min × 3 each\n\nWhy thinking models hit this:\nqwen3.6-35b-a3b and similar 'thinking' / chain-of-thought reasoning models can spend 5–10+ minutes generating reasoning tokens before producing the first body delta the harness considers progress. Streaming output IS happening (so the idle-read timeout would NOT fire), but the harness is counting wall-clock against the entire request. A single hard problem can blow the 15-minute cap legitimately.\n\nWhy arbitrary timeouts are not helping:\n\na) Same value for cloud providers (claude, codex) and local providers (lmstudio, omlx). Local thinking models routinely need \u003e15 min on hard prompts; claude rarely does. Same cap is wrong for both classes.\n\nb) No way to opt out of the cap per provider, per model, per request, per project. /home/erik/Projects/axon/.ddx/config.yaml has no knob.\n\nc) Retries inherit the same cap. A timeout-prone session retries with the same fate. Burns cycles and tokens.\n\nd) The cap defeats the cascade: when a cheap-tier qwen times out, escalation to standard claude is intended — but on a thinking model the timeout doesn't mean 'broken provider', it means 'this prompt is hard'. We then escalate after wasting 15 min, and the standard tier solves the problem in 2. Net effect of cheap-tier-first becomes negative.","events_attachment":"ddx-2c63bb95/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T21:15:18.844737462Z","id":"ddx-2c63bb95","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","area:harness","kind:design-flaw"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-2db5279a","status":"closed","title":"agent: hardcoded 15-minute DefaultProviderRequestTimeout kills thinking-model requests; not configurable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T21:16:13.255743379Z"}
