It still derives `escalationEnabled := spec.Harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 spec.Model == \"\"` instead of mirroring the CLI's `escalate \u0026\u0026 harness == \"\" \u0026\u0026 model == \"\"`. That mismatch is why `ddx work` (default mode, server-submit) still hits \"all tiers exhausted — no viable provider\" with `model_overrides` pointing at non-served models, while `ddx work --local --no-adaptive-min-tier` succeeds. Verified live in this session: `worker-20260429T033325-bedf` failed; `--no-adaptive-min-tier` got `ddx-98e6e9ef` to merge at `115b47c0` on local-tier qwen for zero cost.\n\nSole child: `ddx-c7081f89`. Once that lands, this epic closes.\n\n## Strategic direction (preserved for future reference)\n\nDDx embeds the agent SDK as a Go library to produce traceable execution units: `(bead, input_state) → (output_state, processing_metadata)`. DDx owns the execution unit and its metadata (bead events, cost ledger, escalation summary, evidence directories), and it owns operator policy (`--harness`/`--model`/`--profile`/`--max-cost`/`--no-review`/`--escalate`). It does **not** need to own provider/harness/cost/local-first routing — agent SDK already does that.\n\n| Concern | DDx (now, post-87fb72c2) | Agent SDK |\n|---|---|---|\n| Profile selection | `RouteRequest.Profile` straight to agent | `default/cheap/standard/smart/local/offline/air-gapped/fast/code-smart/code-high` |\n| Per-tier model pinning | opt-in via `--override-model` | `RouteRequest.Model` |\n| Escalation on no-provider | opt-in via `--escalate` (CLI path); **not yet on server worker path** | `escalateProfileLadder` walks `ProfileEscalationLadder=[cheap,standard,smart]` on `ErrNoLiveProvider` |\n| Cost-aware ranking | n/a (delegated) | `internal/routing/score.go` ranks by `CostClass` (local=0 wins) |\n| Adaptive success-rate gating | `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` (sticky 50-window cheap-tier lockout, only consumed via `--escalate`) | `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess map[harness]float64` |\n| Decision trace | bead events `kind:tier-attempt` per RouteCandidate | `RouteDecision.Candidates []RouteCandidate` |\n\n## Optional follow-ups (not in this epic — file separately if/when needed)\n\nThese are no longer blockers; track separately if pursued:\n\n- **Default profile = `local`** so the operator-default honors the local-LLM-first goal without an explicit `--profile local`. Currently default is `default` which maps via project config.\n- **Inject `routing.Inputs.HistoricalSuccess` per-harness** to replace `escalation.AdaptiveMinTier` with agent-native success-rate routing. Frees DDx from maintaining its own success-window state machine.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/agent/profile_ladder.go` helpers** once no caller (CLI or server) consumes them. Currently consumed only via `--escalate`; if `--escalate` is dropped or moved into agent, these can go.\n- **Delete `cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go::AdaptiveMinTier`** once the HistoricalSuccess injection above is in place.\n\n## Out of scope for this epic\n\n- Changes to agent SDK source — if a gap is found, file an upstream bead under `~/Projects/agent/.ddx/`.\n- UI/e2e work — the parent e2e drift epic `ddx-ccdf9cf9` covers that and is independent.\n- Server source changes outside `cli/internal/server/workers.go` — no API endpoint changes, no GraphQL schema changes.","events_attachment":"ddx-3f406266/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3f406266","issue_type":"epic","labels":["area:routing","area:agent","kind:refactor","phase:build"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff contains only execution evidence files (manifest.json, result.json) — no source changes to cli/internal/server/workers.go or anywhere else. None of the AC items can be verified from this diff.","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"routing-unify: delegate provider/harness/cost/local-first routing to agent SDK; ddx preserves execution-unit metadata + operator policy","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:30:06.928832371Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:229:{"acceptance":"- [ ] New command or subcommand: `ddx doctor divergence` or `ddx divergence check` prints the divergence report\n- [ ] Thresholds are configurable via .ddx/config.yaml (default: 5 commits each side, 7 days merge-base age)\n- [ ] execute-loop refuses to start in ERROR state unless --force passed\n- [ ] Structured event 'divergence_status' emitted at loop start and on every doctor run\n- [ ] Optional webhook integration via config.yaml alert_webhook_url\n- [ ] Unit tests: each state (clean, ahead, behind, diverged, network-down, drift)\n- [ ] Integration test: run the loop on a diverged repo and observe the refuse-to-start behavior\n- [ ] Docs: README section on divergence monitoring, thresholds, and how to recover","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:10.525089812Z","description":"Fifth child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The 14-day silent divergence on axon (eitri local 134 commits ahead of merge-base, sindri/origin 336 commits ahead) was caught only because a human happened to try pushing. A trivial monitor running a few times a day would have flagged the problem at 5 commits, not 470.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nAdd a lightweight divergence check that runs either:\n- At the top of each execute-loop iteration (cheap, ~50ms), OR\n- Periodically via a scheduled task (ddx doctor or similar), OR\n- Both\n\nThe check computes:\n- `git fetch origin \u003cbranch\u003e` (tolerant of network failure)\n- local_ahead = `git rev-list --count origin/\u003cbranch\u003e..HEAD`\n- origin_ahead = `git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/\u003cbranch\u003e`\n- merge_base_age = `git log -1 --format=%ar $(git merge-base HEAD origin/\u003cbranch\u003e)`\n\nEmit a structured event 'divergence_status' with these fields. If:\n- local_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- origin_ahead \u003e threshold (default 5): warn\n- both \u003e 0: ERROR — diverged, neither side is a strict ancestor\n- merge_base_age \u003e threshold (default 7 days): warn (drift)\n\nIntegration surface:\n- `ddx doctor` prints the divergence report\n- `ddx agent execute-loop` refuses to start if in the ERROR state (both sides ahead) unless --force is passed\n- An optional webhook / notification channel (config: alert_webhook_url) for ops teams\n\n## Why this is P1 not P0\n\nThe fetch/push/rebase triad (P0) prevents NEW divergence. This bead catches EXISTING divergence and alerts before it grows. Without the P0 fixes, this alert would fire constantly; with them, it should fire rarely. Still valuable as defense in depth.\n\n## Test cases\n\n- Fresh clone: report is clean (0 ahead / 0 behind)\n- Local 3 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=3, no warning\n- Local 6 commits ahead: report shows local_ahead=6, warning emitted\n- Both sides ahead: ERROR status, execute-loop refuses to start\n- Merge base 10 days old with both sides at 0: drift warning, execute-loop continues\n- Network unreachable: report shows 'unknown', no ERROR, execute-loop continues","id":"ddx-42dd982c","issue_type":"task","labels":["execute-loop","monitoring","observability"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: divergence monitoring and alerting","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:12.030389438Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:235:{"acceptance":"- [ ] ddx bead reopen command exists\n- [ ] Status flip + notes append + event recorded atomically\n- [ ] Unit + integration tests\n- [ ] Used by the post-review path from gap #1","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T04:57:51Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"8e0b77a980455d4cea392d42ab4e49b01541bc95","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.573308839Z","description":"Reopening a closed bead after a failing review currently takes 3-4 separate commands: update --status open, update --notes (append), optionally update --description, optionally update --acceptance. This is error-prone and loses the atomicity guarantee that a reopen is a single logical event.\n\n## Scope\n- ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e [--reason \u003ctext\u003e] [--append-notes \u003ctext\u003e]\n- Atomically: set status=open, append a reopen event to the event log, optionally append notes, clear claim fields\n- Emit an event of kind=reopen that shows in ddx bead show\n- Works from CLI and from ddx agent execute-loop review-rejection path\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: first attempt merged, opus review rejected, I had to run three ddx bead update commands plus a description rewrite to get the bead back into a ready-for-second-attempt state. An atomic reopen would have been one command.","events_attachment":"ddx-44084b3e/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:03:51.315684175Z","id":"ddx-44084b3e","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:cli","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-40c2dece","status":"closed","title":"exec: ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e — atomic review-driven reopen","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:04:15.791178942Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:258:{"acceptance":"The epic stays open indefinitely as a backlog for cost-tiered execution and measurement work. Closes ONLY if DDx pivots away from this optimization goal entirely (unlikely). Progress is measured by: (a) count of child beads closed that concretely improved the ladder, reviewed-rejection rate, or measurement granularity; (b) observable reduction in cost-per-closed-bead over time once the metric exists; (c) observable reduction in opus-review rejection rate as the cheap-tier work improves.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c4518bd8b520a204440559044aeecaa799fcf414","created_at":"2026-04-15T03:47:42.980708133Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-4b59b816","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"}],"description":"Standing optimization goal captured 2026-04-15: get the most work done for the least cost, and continuously improve our ability to measure and tune that trade-off.\n\n## Operating principles\n\n1. Cheap models do the work. Local (vidar-coder / qwen3-coder-next / other LM Studio hosts), then budget-tier cloud (claude haiku / gpt-5.4-mini), then standard (claude sonnet / gpt-5.4), then smart (claude opus / gpt-5.4 high-effort). Start cheap; escalate on failure.\n2. Strong models review. Every closed bead gets an opus review against its acceptance. Rejected reviews reopen with specific fixes.\n3. Deterministic fixes are the top rung of the ladder. When LLMs keep failing on a mechanical task, apply the fix manually; capture as input for routing policy.\n4. Measurement feeds optimization. Track per-bead which model attempted, how long, whether it passed review, what failure mode if any.\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (helix review-loop epic from parallel agent)\n\n- ddx-a317208c exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop\n- ddx-dc231144 exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery\n- ddx-fdf031c9 exec: ddx bead review \u003cid\u003e — template a review prompt\n- ddx-44084b3e exec: ddx bead reopen \u003cid\u003e — atomic review-driven reopen\n- ddx-518b2690 exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction\n\n## Existing bead tie-ins (mine)\n\n- ddx-bd0691fe Epic: surface ddx-agent routing visibility (providers/models/check/route-status commands — prereq for routing policy)\n- ddx-bfc1aeaf Visibility 8: capture routing decisions in bead evidence (kind:routing) — feeds the measurement database\n\n## New child beads (to be filed under this epic)\n\nThe beads below are scope for this epic. File as children when we are ready to work each one. NOT pre-filed to avoid blocking the current Svelte+GraphQL migration.\n\n1. Per-bead cost aggregation: extend bead evidence schema to record tokens-in/tokens-out/cost-usd per attempt; add ddx bead metrics aggregate command that rolls up per-bead totals.\n2. Ladder success-rate analytics: ddx agent metrics tier-success — query last N closed beads, report success rate at each tier (vidar-coder, claude-sonnet, claude-opus, deterministic-fix).\n3. Prompt-context pre-filter: shrink the prompt for cheap-tier attempts — omit the full plan doc and include only the bead description + acceptance + 2 most relevant files.\n4. Failure-mode taxonomy: document the known failure modes (vidar long-context hang, read-loop stall, ship-unbuilt-code, opus-shape-miss, etc.) as bead evidence kinds so measurement can aggregate by mode.\n5. Review-rejection telemetry: aggregate opus-review outcomes (APPROVE / APPROVE_WITH_EDITS / REJECT) per originating tier so we know where the shape gaps are.\n6. Dashboard page for the metrics (requires Svelte migration Stage 4 to land first).\n\n## Why this is captured as an epic not a memory-only goal\n\nMemory is read; epics are tracked. Future sessions should see this in ddx bead list and be able to file child beads against it as we discover new measurement needs or optimization opportunities. The memory file is the principle; this epic is the backlog.","events_attachment":"ddx-4b59b816/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-4b59b816","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:optimization","area:agent","area:cli","area:measurement","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"session_id":"eb-2dcc8231","status":"closed","title":"Epic: cost-tiered execution + measurement — maximize closed-bead throughput per dollar","updated_at":"2026-04-28T22:37:12.898039753Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:266:{"acceptance":"AC1. When an execute-bead session exits without committing in the worktree AND without writing .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/no_changes_rationale.txt, the harness records a NEW dedicated status (e.g. 'agent_silent_exit' or 'no_evidence_produced') instead of letting ddx-land-coordinator land an iteration containing only execution evidence.\n\nAC2. The codex reviewer's BLOCK rationale ('change set only adds execution metadata files') becomes redundant — the harness catches this case before the reviewer is even invoked, saving a redundant review pass and its cost.\n\nAC3. The execute-bead prompt template is updated so the rationale-on-incomplete-work instruction is harder to skip — ideally a tool the agent must invoke (e.g. SubmitNoChangesRationale tool) rather than 'write a file and exit'.\n\nAC4. When the agent did run Edit tool calls but the worktree has no commit, the harness logs a warning naming each file the agent edited that is now uncommitted, so operators can recover the work or diagnose the silent-commit-failure.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the agent-self-commit contract, the rationale-file fallback, and the new 'agent_silent_exit' classification.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3967690","closing_commit_sha":"cedf316fc6d5082eff11bff2f38ee5618958b20f","created_at":"2026-04-30T00:10:22.985451193Z","description":"Observed today across 5 attempts on the axon repo (claude/sonnet, claude/opus, codex/gpt-5.4-mini): the agent does real work in the worktree (multiple Edit tool calls touching real source files) but never runs git commit. Result: iteration commit is built by ddx-land-coordinator with ONLY execution-evidence files (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json, manifest.json, usage.json), no actual code or doc changes. The post-merge codex reviewer correctly flags BLOCK with rationale 'change set only adds execution metadata files'.\n\nConcrete evidence (axon repo, 2026-04-29):\n\n  Attempt: 20260429T235353-d14f6b91 (axon-80979cb8 / A1 sub-bead)\n  Harness: claude / model: sonnet\n  Duration: 817 sec, cost $3.61, 132 tool calls\n  Tool breakdown: 79 Bash, 34 Read, 11 Grep, 8 Edit\n  Edit targets: ui/src/lib/api.ts, ui/src/lib/policy-evaluator.ts,\n    ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte, ui/tests/e2e/link-policy-retry.spec.ts\n  Iteration commit (ddx-land-coordinator): contains exactly 2 files —\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/result.json (23 lines)\n    .ddx/executions/20260429T235353-d14f6b91/usage.json (9 lines)\n  Net: zero actual edits landed despite $3.61 of model time spent making them.\n\nThe same pattern appeared on:\n  20260429T235406-20eeaf2c (axon-db7a6d0a, claude/sonnet, $0.57, 4.2 min, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190657-fa91f49b (axon-ab2e52e0, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.08, 24s, stalled)\n  20260429T190634-579df880 (axon-80979cb8, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.12, 104s, BLOCK)\n  20260429T190646-5aa5b46e (axon-db7a6d0a, openrouter/gpt-5.4-mini, $0.06, 25s, BLOCK)\n\nTotal burn today: ~$4.50 across 5 attempts on 3 beads, 0 closed.\n\nThe execute-bead prompt template tells the agent to commit:\n  '4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with git add \u003cspecific-paths\u003e...'\n\nSo agent self-commit is the contract. But:\n\n(a) When typecheck/lint/test fails, the agent is told to fix or write no_changes_rationale.txt — but apparently many agents are bailing silently without writing the rationale either, so the harness sees a clean worktree (no diff staged or committed) and treats it as 'agent made no commits'. The iteration then carries only the auto-generated execution evidence.\n\n(b) There may also be a path where the agent commits but a pre-commit hook silently rejects it. Have not confirmed; the worktree was reaped before I could inspect.\n\nThe issue spans multiple harnesses (claude, codex via embedded openrouter), so it is not specific to one provider's commit semantics. Either:\n  - The harness should reject 'agent made no commits AND no no_changes_rationale.txt' as a separate failure class instead of silently letting ddx-land-coordinator land an empty iteration\n  - The agent prompt should make rationale-writing on incomplete work non-optional with stronger language\n  - The harness should run a final-stage check 'did the agent produce ANY signal — commit OR rationale — and if not, treat as harness-execution-failed and surface to the operator'","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:43:25.451101407Z","id":"ddx-4e0a4a59","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:harness","area:execute-bead","kind:correctness"],"owner":"erik","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: agents make edits but skip git commit; iteration commits land only execution evidence","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:43:46.370829758Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:275:{"acceptance":"- [ ] --output flag accepts text | json-result | session-jsonl\n- [ ] Default is text\n- [ ] Integration test covers all three modes\n- [ ] No behavior change for session-jsonl mode","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:26:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"cbaee23432d7fe8167b5dd1974fc237dc20437a4","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.719835899Z","description":"ddx agent run currently prints the agent's full session (JSONL events) to stdout regardless of flags. To extract just the final assistant text message, callers have to parse JSONL and walk the session log. This breaks trivial automation like 'review=$(ddx agent run ... --prompt @/tmp/r.md)'.\n\n## Scope\n- --output text: emit only the final assistant text message to stdout (no tool calls, no JSONL)\n- --output json-result: emit only the result-type JSON object (session metadata + final text)\n- --output session-jsonl: current behavior; keep as non-default opt-in\n- Default switches to --output text\n\n## Context\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop: both review runs spilled 90-200KB of session JSONL to stdout, requiring python3 JSON-walking to extract the ~2KB opus verdict. --output text would have made the review invocation one line instead of three.","events_attachment":"ddx-518b2690/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:36:40.703736961Z","id":"ddx-518b2690","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:cli","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-dca40d97","status":"closed","title":"exec: ddx agent run --output text — plain result extraction","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:36:54.864845558Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:280:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-30T03:39:31.542862754Z","description":"Finding from fresh-eyes review of the run/try/work boundary plan.\n\nProblem:\nThe plan says there should be one simple run command layered under try and work, but current governing docs and shipped skill references still describe ddx agent run, ddx agent execute-bead, execute-loop, quorum harness flags, and agent-provider command surfaces as active user/automation primitives. Examples found during review include FEAT-006 Agent Service overview/responsibilities, FEAT-010 Layer 1 wording, and cli/internal/skills/ddx/reference/agents.md persona/quorum/help examples. Parallel copied skill docs under .agents/skills/ddx and .claude/skills/ddx contain the same stale vocabulary.\n\nWhy this matters:\nThe docs are executable guidance for agents. Leaving legacy command names and flags in skills will keep agents routing work through the old surfaces even if the implementation adds top-level run/try/work.\n\nRequired fix:\nMake the public command taxonomy bright-line and uniform across feature docs, skill docs, and CLI help: ddx run is the single agent execution primitive, ddx try layers retry/review policy over run, and ddx work layers bead orchestration over try. Agent-internal service commands may exist only as hidden/internal plumbing and must not be documented as workflow commands.\n\nAcceptance:\n- FEAT-006 and FEAT-010 no longer describe ddx agent run/execute-bead/execute-loop as public orchestration commands.\n- DDx skill references in cli/internal/skills/ddx, .agents/skills/ddx, and .claude/skills/ddx use ddx run/try/work consistently.\n- Quorum/compare/persona examples are either migrated to the simplified surface or removed if they imply DDx harness routing.\n- A docs/skill grep test or validation step fails on stale public references to ddx agent run, execute-bead, execute-loop, --harnesses, or provider-routing examples outside explicit migration notes.","id":"ddx-52ce6249","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","area:docs","area:cli"],"parent":"ddx-deb9be7e","priority":1,"spec_id":"review:power-passthrough-boundary-stale-commands","status":"closed","title":"review finding: remove stale ddx agent run and execute-bead command guidance","updated_at":"2026-04-30T03:50:03.186901956Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:301:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36.597150715Z","description":"'ddx agent doctor' currently reports a harness as OK if its binary exists on PATH. This is insufficient: for the embedded 'agent' harness, the actual backend is a remote inference server (LM Studio on bragi/vidar/grendel or a cloud API). A healthy binary + unreachable backend reports OK but cannot do any work.\n\n## Observed failure\n\nDuring the axon ADR-018 execute-loop, 'ddx agent doctor' reported '10/10 harnesses available' while bragi's LM Studio was stopped. Trying to run ddx-agent -provider bragi returned a 502 Bad Gateway. I had to manually curl each host to discover the real state.\n\n## Scope\n\n- Extend 'ddx agent doctor' to probe each provider in the agent config with a small 'models' or 'ping' request within a short timeout\n- Report per-provider status alongside per-harness status: provider name, host, last-probe result, last-probe ms, last-probe error\n- --quick mode: skip provider probes (current behavior), for fast checks\n- Default behavior: probe providers; show a two-column status (harness OK, provider reachable Y/N)\n- Integration with execute-loop's discovery-driven escalation (ddx-dc231144): the probe result feeds the per-provider cooldown map\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] doctor probes every provider in the agent config by default\n- [ ] Unreachable/5xx providers are reported as degraded, not OK\n- [ ] doctor output is machine-readable via --json\n- [ ] doctor --quick keeps the old fast-path behavior\n- [ ] Integration test: doctor against a fake provider that returns 502 reports the provider as unhealthy","events_attachment":"ddx-5bac3c6b/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: harness doctor must probe provider backends, not just binaries","updated_at":"2026-04-15T03:45:01.910986192Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:360:{"acceptance":"- [ ] cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go Merge function is replaced (or supplemented) with a Rebase function that takes (dir, base, head, onto) and rebases head onto onto starting from base\n- [ ] On clean rebase, master fast-forwards to the rebased tip; one commit per bead\n- [ ] On conflict, commits are preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ via the existing iterationRef helper, the bead is unclaimed, the worker loop stops (same shape as the fetch-divergence path)\n- [ ] The 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commit is removed entirely; a dirty worktree at landing time is a hard error with a structured message\n- [ ] No code path in the execute-loop ever calls `git merge --no-edit` or any merge strategy — verified by grep\n- [ ] LAND-LOCK REQUIREMENT: while a rebase + ff-push sequence is in progress for a given projectRoot, no other rebase + ff-push sequence may run against the same projectRoot. The serialization point can be (a) a per-project flock on .git/ddx-land.lock, (b) a single goroutine in the ddx server process that owns all rebase-land operations for that project (preferred — this matches the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 coordinator design), or (c) an equivalent mutex held across fetch → rebase → ff-push. Without this, two workers whose rebases complete simultaneously will both try to advance master to different SHAs and one will lose. The test for this requirement: spawn N (N\u003e=5) concurrent land calls on the same projectRoot and assert that (i) exactly one commit is on master per submission after all calls return, (ii) the submissions are processed in some total order (not a set of parallel races), (iii) any submission that loses the race is either retried against the new tip OR preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/ with a clear reason — never silently dropped\n- [ ] Unit test: two sibling workers, second rebases onto first's tip, master has exactly 2 new commits in linear order with no merge parents\n- [ ] Unit test: conflicting rebase preserves commits and emits the land_conflict status\n- [ ] Unit test: dirty worktree at landing time errors out without producing a checkpoint commit\n- [ ] Integration test: running 5 parallel workers on the same machine for 10 iterations produces exactly 50 linear commits on master with no merge parents anywhere in the reflog\n\nThis bead is subsumed by the meta-bead ddx-8746d8a6 \"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end\". The coordinator pattern in the meta-bead IS the implementation of this bead's rebase + land-lock requirements. If ddx-8746d8a6 lands first, close this bead as satisfied by the coordinator's rebase step. If this bead lands first, its implementation should be the foundation for the coordinator.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:20:11.501071514Z","description":"Third child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. When two workers run in parallel on the same machine (each in its own execute-bead worktree), and both complete and attempt to land, the orchestrator uses `git merge --no-edit` with the ort strategy to fold one worker's commits into master. This produces a merge commit tree instead of a linear history, and triggers repeated 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits when the working tree isn't clean.\n\n## Evidence (source)\n\ncli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 defines the Merge function:\n\n    // Merge merges rev into dir's working tree via git merge. It commits any...\n    func Merge(dir, rev string) error {\n        _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"commit\", \"-m\", \"chore: checkpoint before merge\").Run()\n        ...\n        out, err := osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--no-edit\", rev).CombinedOutput()\n        if err != nil {\n            _ = osexec.Command(\"git\", \"-C\", dir, \"merge\", \"--abort\").Run()\n            return fmt.Errorf(\"merge: %s: %w\", strings.TrimSpace(string(out)), err)\n        }\n    }\n\n## Evidence (symptom on axon)\n\ngit reflog master on axon's eitri machine shows 12+ `Merge made by the 'ort' strategy` entries on 2026-04-13 within a 2-hour window, each one merging a sibling SHA that never appeared on origin:\n\n    master@{8}  merge 5eb67fd0afab40ba4f9b06a8228760d69187af9d\n    master@{12} merge 77999324c59bb715ee6ef290255a5b31e17b1930\n    master@{13} merge 79772765312e3bc5a0715a6d9a2860016463e594\n    master@{17} merge 268f63283af2df6bfde94200b5159222ea4f996d\n    ... and nine more\n\nInterleaved with 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits. This is the signature of two+ parallel workers each producing sibling branches that get merged back into master one at a time, each merge preceded by a defensive checkpoint.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nReplace merge-based landing with rebase-based landing:\n\n1. When a worker completes, its worktree is at commit W (child of base B)\n2. Between B and now, master may have advanced to M (due to sibling workers or fetch-from-origin)\n3. Instead of `git merge --no-edit W` on master, do:\n   a. `git rebase --onto M B W` in the worker's worktree (or equivalent plumbing via `git cherry-pick B..W` onto M)\n   b. On conflict: preserve the worker's commits under refs/ddx/iterations/\u003cbead\u003e/\u003ctimestamp\u003e/ (existing infrastructure at execute_bead_orchestrator.go:21-26), unclaim the bead, stop the worker loop\n   c. On clean rebase: fast-forward master to the rebased tip\n4. NO checkpoint commits. If the master worktree is dirty, that's a bug in the preceding workflow (should have been committed by the worker) — fail loudly, don't paper over with a checkpoint\n\n## Why rebase over merge\n\n- Linear history — no fan of merge parents from parallel runs\n- Conflicts surface earlier (during rebase, not as a semantically-broken merge)\n- Each bead produces exactly one commit on master; a clean log of 'this bead → this commit'\n- Push-ff-only from the sibling push bead (ddx-83ec1430) works because history stays linear\n\n## Interaction with fetch bead\n\nWith ddx-bd42f640 ensuring HEAD is current with origin before work starts, and this bead ensuring intra-machine landings are linear, a worker's commit sequence is always: fetch → linear base → work → rebase → push. No merges, no sibling fans.\n\n## Remove the checkpoint-before-merge pattern\n\nThe 'chore: checkpoint before merge' commits exist because the Merge function tries to handle a dirty worktree by committing it first. In the rebase model, a dirty worktree is illegal — the worker must have committed everything before the landing phase. If the worktree is dirty at landing time, error out immediately with a clear message about the worker not having committed cleanly. Do not silently checkpoint.","id":"ddx-6aa50e57","issue_type":"bug","labels":["bug","execute-loop","git","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-43009cc","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: rebase sibling workers instead of git merge --no-edit (ort)","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:26:48.546705773Z"}

Never leave beads half-owned.\n- Testing expectations for bead implementations (not AC-level, but default guidance): favor stubs over mocks when a collaborator behavior matters to the test; exercise all layers where the project has them (unit / integration / e2e); measure coverage only where the project tracks it — do not introduce coverage tooling just for this.\n- CLI reference: ddx work, ddx agent execute-bead \u003cid\u003e, ddx agent execute-loop, with a note that ddx work wraps execute-loop.\n\nSource material: existing /skills/ddx-run/SKILL.md. Keep the lifecycle enforcement (claim → implement → verify → close). Strip TDD-as-mandatory framing if present (TDD is a methodology opinion; the ddx-native stance is 'tests must be included and must run', not a specific methodology).\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/reference/work.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (separate bead ddx-bd852e86)\n- Other reference/*.md files (separate beads)\n- Testing persona work (Phase 3)","id":"ddx-6aa90265","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/reference/work.md — drain queue, execute-bead, verify+close","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:23:03.221075141Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.\n\n## Gaps in the current skill\n\n1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:\n   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')\n   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')\n   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.\n   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)\n\n2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.\n\n3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.\n\n4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.\n\n5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.\n\n6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.\n\n7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.\n\n## Scope\n\nUpdate skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:\n\n- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above\n- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline\n- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic\n- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection\n- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:\n  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text\n  - Not naming file paths in the description\n  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command\n  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed\n\nKeep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.\n- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.\n- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section\n- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections\n- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented\n- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added\n- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated\n- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}

The WorkerManager in cli/internal/server/workers.go already tracks this (WorkerRecord.CurrentBead at line 51, WorkerRecord.State, WorkerRecord.Harness, WorkerRecord.Model, WorkerRecord.Attempts/Successes/Failures) but it is only accessible through the server API. Local --local workers are not tracked by the server at all.\n\nThis bead adds ddx agent workers as a top-level CLI subcommand. It must not depend on worktrunk (wt) or any external tool.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. New command: ddx agent workers (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nRegister alongside the existing ddx agent log command. Flags:\n  --json         emit raw JSON array\n  --watch        re-render every 2s (like watch -n2) until Ctrl-C\n  --project      filter to a specific project root (default: CWD project)\n\n### 2. Data sources (two paths)\nA. Server-submitted workers: GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/api/agent/workers — deserialize []WorkerRecord from the server. Already implemented in cli/internal/server/workers.go List() / HTTP handler.\nB. Local --local workers: the server does not track these. Detect them by:\n   1. Reading .ddx/workers/*/status.json files in the project root (server writes these even for local workers when they self-register — confirm in workers.go; if not, fall back to scanning active execute-bead worktrees)\n   2. Scanning .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-* directories (from ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go line 403) and reading the manifest.json inside each to extract bead_id, attempt_id, harness, model, started_at\n\n### 3. Local worker detection via manifest scan\nFor each directory matching .ddx/.execute-bead-wt-*:\n  - Read .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/manifest.json (path encoded in worktree name suffix)\n  - Check if worktree is still active (git worktree list --porcelain)\n  - Read execute-loop-heartbeat-at from bead.Extra (requires ddx-7651506a) if available\n  - Derive synthetic WorkerRecord from manifest fields\n\n### 4. Table output (default)\nColumns: STATE | BEAD | TITLE (truncated 40 chars) | HARNESS | MODEL | ELAPSED | ATTEMPTS\n- STATE: running (green), exited (dim), failed (red)\n- ELAPSED: human-readable (e.g., 4m32s)\n- If current bead title unavailable (local worker pre-manifest), show bead ID\n\n### 5. Merge and deduplicate\nWhen both server and manifest sources are available, deduplicate by worker_id or attempt_id. Server record takes precedence over manifest-derived record for the same worker.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — add newAgentWorkersCommand(), register in newAgentCommand()\n- cli/internal/server/workers.go — WorkerRecord struct (lines 31-54), List API\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadWtPrefix constant (line 403), manifest shape (lines 726-771)\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry Correlation map (bead_id field)","events_attachment":"ddx-9060c8b2/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T07:45:03.73510309Z","id":"ddx-9060c8b2","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","execute-loop","observability","cli"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-3f16c49","status":"closed","title":"Add ddx agent workers command — native live worker status with current bead","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:47.499704451Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:521:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer using the web UI on a real-world-sized workspace (tens of projects × hundreds of beads each), the beads list opens in under a second and the cross-project all-beads view stays usable. Before any perf fix lands, we can reproduce the problem and measure improvement — not guess.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Perf harness exists and is reusable.**\n   - Fixture builder generates `N × M` beads across projects with configurable shape (status mix, label density, dep count). Reuses the real `bead.Store` write path (no fake writer).\n   - Benchmark covers at minimum: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject`, `beads` (cross-project), `docGraph`, `sessions`. Adding a new target is a single-table-row change.\n   - Each target produces p50/p95/p99 for in-process calls and end-to-end HTTP. Results write to a human-readable report (markdown or JSON) plus a machine-readable artifact suitable for CI diffing later.\n\n2. **Baseline recorded.** First run of the harness on the current `main` binary is stored in the bead notes and in `docs/helix/04-observe/perf/YYYY-MM-DD-baseline.md` (or equivalent existing location). Numbers include hardware + go version footer.\n\n3. **BeadsByProject scope fix.** `BeadsByProject` calls a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, …)` that opens exactly one project's store. Unit test asserts only that project's store is opened (no iteration over other projects).\n\n4. **Filter pushdown.** Status/label/projectID filters apply at the per-file / per-entry parse boundary, not post-materialization. Benchmark captures before/after for the common filtered case (e.g., `status=open`).\n\n5. **Perf targets on fixture (10 projects × 500 beads = 5000 beads total):**\n   - `bead(id:)` p95 ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP (already covered by `ddx-ad0db8fd`; this bead may inherit the work).\n   - `beadsByProject(first=50)` p95 ≤ 75ms in-process / ≤ 250ms HTTP.\n   - `beads(first=50, no projectID)` p95 ≤ 200ms in-process / ≤ 500ms HTTP.\n   These are targets for the **first** pass (scope + pushdown). If they are not met with (1)+(2), escalate to the in-memory index (step 3 of Part 2 in the description) and update these targets with the new baseline.\n\n6. **Stale-read safety.** Regardless of fix chosen, a test writes a bead via `ddx bead update`, then queries it via GraphQL, then asserts the new value is visible on the very next request. Works under concurrency: N concurrent readers + writers interleaved without torn reads or panics.\n\n7. **CI posture (soft).** The harness is runnable via `make bench-graphql` (or the project's equivalent). CI wiring to fail on \u003e25% regression is a follow-up bead — recorded in this bead's notes with the proposed threshold but not implemented here.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** Opening `/beads` on the fixture project returns an interactive (clickable) list within 1s. Opening the cross-project `/beads` page returns within 2s. These are ceilings, not p95 targets — guards against outright regression, not fine-grained perf.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead's notes list `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions shard/index) as the other two bead/session perf threads that should inherit this harness.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7a2078a59d9885cb94f4c188ea95ce5149f969cd","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:41:52.271330549Z","description":"## Observed\n\nPer-project `/beads` page is noticeably slow; the cross-project \"all beads\" view is substantially slower. Related: `ddx-ad0db8fd` already identified the single-bead-detail slowness caused by the same storage pattern.\n\n## Root cause (same pattern, different call site)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:22,40` —\n\n- `Beads` (cross-project) and `BeadsByProject` (single-project) both call `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search)`.\n- `GetBeadSnapshots` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105`) iterates every registered project, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), filters in Go. Cost is `O(projects × beads_per_project)` on every call, independent of the filter/page size.\n- All filtering (status, label, project, search) is Go-side, post-read. The page-size window is applied in `beadConnectionFromSnapshots` after the full corpus is already materialized.\n- Cross-project \"all beads\" = same loop, just without the projectID filter. It is the worst case by design.\n\nThis is the same shape that's breaking the Sessions feed (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) and single-bead detail (`ddx-ad0db8fd`). A point fix here is insufficient — we need a measurement surface first so we stop relying on anecdote.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Perf harness (foundational; enables everything else)\n\nIntroduce a reusable GraphQL perf harness under `cli/internal/server/perf/` or `cli/internal/server/graphql/bench_test.go`:\n\n- Seeded fixture builder: generates N projects × M beads with configurable distribution of status/labels/dependencies. Reuses existing store primitives (no fake data layer).\n- Go benchmark + scriptable HTTP runner (wrapper around an already-running server) reporting p50/p95/p99 per query shape.\n- Targets covered on day one: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject(projectID, first, after, status, label, search)`, `beads(projectID=nil, first, after, status, label)`, `docGraph`, `sessions` (so the sessions shard work `ddx-2ceb02fa` inherits this harness). New query targets are added by writing a single table row.\n- Thresholds are **recorded, not asserted**, in the first pass. CI surfaces a regression if a threshold is breached beyond a configured delta (e.g., +25% p95). Initial pass is \"land the harness and record baseline\"; a follow-up wires it into CI.\n\n### Part 2 — Fix the scan pattern\n\nMinimum viable, in priority order:\n\n1. **Scope by project when the query is scoped.** `BeadsByProject` should call a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, filters…)` that only opens that one project's store. Trivial change; eliminates the cross-project factor for the common case.\n2. **Early filter pushdown.** `GetBeadSnapshots` should apply status/label/projectID filters at the file-parse boundary where cheap, not after materializing every bead. (Still reads every file — deeper fix below.)\n3. **Lightweight in-memory index with invalidation.** After (1) and (2), if \"all beads\" or search is still slow, introduce an index keyed `{projectID, beadID}` with basic metadata (status, labels, title, updatedAt). Refresh on bead write + debounced filesystem watch. Full-body reads stay lazy — the index never holds bodies. This is the same storage discipline we adopted for sessions in `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\n\nThe harness from Part 1 is the gate for choosing between (2) and (3); don't commit to the index without numbers.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full-text search infrastructure (tracked separately if needed).\n- Changing the on-disk bead format.\n- Cross-node federation performance. Current concern is a single server reading a single workspace.","events_attachment":"ddx-9ce6842a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"stalled: read_only_tools_exceeded","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T10:04:40Z","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-9ce6842a","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy\n\nTwo standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):\n\n1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.\n\n2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.\n\nThese directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies \"optimize in place no matter what.\" If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.\n\n**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the \"cross-project\" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.\n**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. The baseline `.md` and code comments mention them, but no `ddx bead update` call or notes update appears in the diff. If the notes were updated via a separate mechanism not captured in this branch, this finding can be dismissed.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Baseline and perf-thread cross-reference\n\nBaseline recorded in `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and JSON sibling from the 10 projects x 500 beads fixture (5,000 beads total, 1,000 session rows). Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.\n\nKey p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.\n\nCross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.\n\nReview follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path. Proposed CI posture remains a follow-up +25% p95 regression gate, not implemented in this bead.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Baseline refresh after session-index fixture alignment\n\nRe-ran `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` after updating the perf fixture to seed current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy `sessions.jsonl`. Refreshed artifacts: `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and `.json`.\n\nRefreshed p95 results: `bead` 7.23ms in-process / 7.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 7.18ms in-process / 7.83ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.49ms in-process / 67.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.74ms in-process / 7.73ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.45ms in-process / 14.19ms HTTP.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Final `make bench-graphql` verification\n\nAfter fixing `cli/Makefile` to pass an absolute `DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR`, `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` writes to the intended repo docs path. Final p95 results from that run: `bead` 6.76ms in-process / 7.20ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 6.88ms in-process / 7.85ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 63.22ms in-process / 71.82ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.75ms in-process / 7.56ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.24ms in-process / 13.64ms HTTP.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nReview payload is insufficient: the only commit shown is the execution-evidence chore. Re-run the review against the full branch diff (commits `9dc0a408` and any predecessors introducing the harness, `BeadsByProject` scope fix, filter pushdown, `make bench-graphql`, baseline markdown, and Playwright specs) so each AC can be graded against real code.\nExecution events in `manifest.json` also record a prior iter-1 finding (Playwright cross-project test in `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigated to the per-project URL rather than the all-beads route) and a notes-cross-reference gap; confirm both are resolved in the full diff before approving AC §8 and §9.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-ace9ade3","status":"closed","title":"Bead list and cross-project all-beads are too slow: establish a GraphQL perf harness and fix N·M scan pattern","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:36:55.005145669Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:535:{"acceptance":"- [ ] execute-loop runs post-merge review by default\n- [ ] Review tier auto-selected (max(impl_tier+1, smart))\n- [ ] Review verdict JSON is stable and documented\n- [ ] APPROVE closes bead with review evidence attached\n- [ ] REQUEST_CHANGES reopens bead via ddx bead reopen and triggers next tier\n- [ ] BLOCK stops escalation and flags for human\n- [ ] --no-review opt-out works; 'review:skip' label works\n- [ ] Integration test: impossible-AC bead produces REQUEST_CHANGES forever without infinite-looping (bounded by max-tier)","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T05:36:55Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"3c149a123610788c014051e0bae082cb89aee69d","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.524036278Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-a317208c","depends_on_id":"ddx-fdf031c9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-a317208c","depends_on_id":"ddx-518b2690","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"}],"description":"execute-loop currently closes a bead on merge. There is no automated post-merge review step validating the commit against the bead's AC. Real-world practice requires a second-opinion agent (typically a higher-capability tier) to produce APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.\n\n## Principle\n\nReview is on-by-default with zero flags. The tool picks a tier \u003e= the implementation tier (defaulting to smart). The user overrides only in exceptional cases.\n\n## Default flow\n\n- Implementation agent runs at current tier (see ddx-dc231144 tier-based escalation)\n- On successful merge, tool automatically renders a review prompt via 'ddx bead review' (ddx-fdf031c9)\n- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier + 1, smart)\n- Review result is structured JSON: { verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | BLOCK, findings: [...] }\n- APPROVE → close bead, attach review evidence\n- REQUEST_CHANGES → reopen bead via 'ddx bead reopen' (ddx-44084b3e), append review body, let the tier-escalation policy pick the next attempt\n- BLOCK → stop escalating, flag for human\n\n## Rare overrides\n\n- --no-review (skip review entirely — e.g., doc-only beads in a tight loop)\n- --review-harness / --review-model (explicit reviewer override for debug)\n- Per-bead opt-out via a 'review:skip' label on the bead\n\n## Dependencies\n\n- ddx-fdf031c9 (ddx bead review command) provides the review prompt template\n- ddx-44084b3e (ddx bead reopen) provides the atomic reopen primitive\n- ddx-518b2690 (plain --output text) makes extracting the review verdict trivial\n- ddx-5bac3c6b (provider health probe) feeds the reviewer tier selection\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n- qwen3-coder-next merged A1 with real AC gaps (missing round-trip tests, zero Postgres coverage, scope creep)\n- Without review, the bead would have closed silently and the gaps would surface only at epic rollup — much later and much more expensive to fix\n- Review took 41s + a few minutes of reading. Cheap relative to the real cost of shipping broken code downstream.","events_attachment":"ddx-a317208c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T05:57:25.094183105Z","id":"ddx-a317208c","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"\u003coperator-feedback 2026-04-15\u003e\nCritical gap discovered during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n\nWhen a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the REVIEW FINDINGS must be surfaced to the next execute-bead attempt. Today this fails silently because:\n\n1. Review findings posted via 'ddx bead update --notes' are NOT included in the execute-bead prompt.md. Execute-bead threads only \u003cdescription\u003e and \u003cacceptance\u003e into the XML payload.\n\n2. Therefore a reopen-via-notes does NOT communicate the review findings to the next agent. The agent reads the original description + AC, sees the existing code already matches the description, and declares 'already_satisfied' — auto-closing the bead.\n\n3. The workaround I had to use was updating the DESCRIPTION directly with the review findings. But then the description is no longer a stable spec — it's a moving target carrying review history.\n\n## Required behavior\n\nWhen a bead is reopened with a review verdict attached, execute-bead's generated prompt MUST include the most recent REQUEST_CHANGES review findings as a distinct section, e.g.:\n\n  \u003creview-findings iteration=\"2\" reviewer=\"claude-opus-4-6\"\u003e\n  BLOCKING: UserIdentity field 'subject' should be 'external_id' per ADR-018 §3\n  PARTIAL: aud-array test asserts serde_json::Error instead of AuthError::CredentialMalformed\n  ...\n  \u003c/review-findings\u003e\n\nAnd the execute-bead instructions should read: 'If \u003creview-findings\u003e is present, address each BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Do not declare already_satisfied if any BLOCKING finding is unaddressed.'\n\n## Observed failure mode\n\nSonnet at iter 2 of axon-43ed769b read the prompt (which had the original description with 'subject' and the unchanged AC), verified all type shapes match, ran tests green, and wrote a no_changes_rationale declaring the work complete. The 'already_satisfied' close path auto-closed the bead. The actual BLOCKING issue (wrong field name) was invisible to the execute-bead layer. I had to manually reopen and rewrite the description with fix-up instructions inline.\n\n## This should be part of the post-review epic scope\n\nMaking review findings round-trip through the prompt is half of what 'post-review agent in execute-loop' means. Adding to this bead's scope:\n\n- Review verdict JSON includes a 'findings' array with severity (blocking|partial|nit) and location\n- Reopen path persists the findings to the bead\n- execute-bead prompt rendering pulls latest review findings and injects them as a distinct XML section\n- Execute-bead instructions teach the agent to treat blocking findings as first-class work items\n- no_changes / already_satisfied paths check for unaddressed blocking findings before closing\n\u003c/operator-feedback\u003e","owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-451c8bc5","status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge review agent on-by-default in execute-loop","updated_at":"2026-04-15T05:57:50.41702859Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:552:{"acceptance":"- [ ] claude_stream.go:runClaudeStreaming uses opts.SessionLogDir when non-empty, falling back to r.Config.SessionLogDir (matches agent_runner.go:136-138 pattern)\n- [ ] A claude-harness execute-bead run writes agent-\u003csessionID\u003e.jsonl into the bundle's embedded/ dir, not .ddx/agent-logs/\n- [ ] Unit test covers the override precedence for the claude path\n- [ ] Regression test: existing claude runs without execute-bead context still write to DefaultLogDir\n- [ ] Any other harnesses that emit JSONL traces (gemini, codex, etc.) audited and brought to the same override-respecting behavior","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T06:03:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"f56dbe210daec8b83f0b857d6b1278ca18bb41c2","created_at":"2026-04-15T18:59:11.996858451Z","description":"The embedded ddx-agent harness honors the per-run SessionLogDir override (agent_runner.go:136-138: `logDir := opts.SessionLogDir; if empty fall back to r.Config.SessionLogDir`) so that execute-bead runs drop their JSONL trace inside the bundle at .ddx/executions/\u003cattempt\u003e/embedded/agent-*.jsonl.\n\nThe claude harness does NOT honor that override. claude_stream.go:327 reads only r.Config.SessionLogDir, so every claude execute-bead run writes its trace into the runner-wide default (`.ddx/agent-logs/`) and the bundle's embedded/ dir stays empty. execute_bead.go:446 already passes SessionLogDir: embeddedStateDir through RunOptions; it just isn't used by the claude path.\n\nEmpirical evidence from axon's 2026-04-15 ADR-018 session: 2 runs with `harness: agent` produced 34-51 MB traces in embedded/; 23 runs with `harness: claude` left embedded/ empty while `.ddx/agent-logs/` grew to 162 MB.\n\nFix: plumb opts.SessionLogDir into runClaudeStreaming (either accept it as a parameter or read it off resolvedOpts) and prefer it over r.Config.SessionLogDir when non-empty. Matches the embedded harness behavior exactly.\n\nParity matters: users want per-run forensic traces attached to the bundle regardless of which harness ran the bead.","events_attachment":"ddx-a97b10cd/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-18T06:16:55.928020972Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"execution_failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"","id":"ddx-a97b10cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","area:agent","area:observability","kind:parity"],"owner":"erik","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-a0988d58","status":"closed","title":"claude harness: write per-iteration trace into execution bundle's embedded/ dir","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:17:16.973189205Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:580:{"acceptance":"- [ ] docs/EXECUTE-LOOP.md exists and covers all four sections above (topology, invariants, runbook, troubleshooting)\n- [ ] README links to the execute-loop doc from the agent command section\n- [ ] Runbook includes explicit recovery steps for the 'both sides diverged' scenario that axon is currently in\n- [ ] The doc is reviewed by someone who did NOT write it before landing\n- [ ] A new operator can follow the runbook to add a secondary machine without assistance\n- [ ] Existing patterns (checkpoint commits, ort merges) are documented as legacy and expected to disappear post-P0-beads","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:36.832469975Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-83ec1430","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b0374901","depends_on_id":"ddx-6aa50e57","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Sixth and final child of ddx-b7ee0d6f. The axon divergence happened partly because the multi-machine topology was never written down. Two machines (eitri and sindri) both ran execute-loop against the same project, neither was designated authoritative, and nobody had defined the pull/push contract. The result was predictable once you look at it, but there was no document that said 'this is wrong' before it happened.\n\n## Required documentation\n\nWrite docs/EXECUTE-LOOP.md (or expand the ddx README) covering:\n\n### Topology models\n\n**Single-machine solo**: one machine, one execute-loop. No coordination needed beyond the fetch/push/rebase triad (ddx-bd42f640, ddx-83ec1430, ddx-6aa50e57).\n\n**Primary + secondary(s)**: one machine is authoritative (pushes to origin). Secondaries pull-only — they can run the execute-loop if and only if the fetch bead guarantees they fast-forward from origin before every bead, and they refuse to land any work locally. Useful for running review/eval passes in parallel without forking.\n\n**Peer mesh**: multiple machines all push to origin. Each must implement the fetch → verify ancestry → rebase → push invariant from the P0 beads. Requires the tracker concurrency bead (ddx-2791dc4f) to be solved or all machines will step on each other's beads.jsonl.\n\n### Invariants\n\n- Before claiming a bead: local HEAD must be a strict ancestor of origin/\u003cdefault-branch\u003e (or equal). If not, refuse to claim.\n- After landing a bead successfully: push --ff-only or mark land_conflict and stop. Never leave work trapped locally.\n- Between sibling workers on the same machine: rebase onto the latest master, never merge.\n- Every bead claim must be visible to every other execute-loop before the claimer starts work (tracker concurrency).\n\n### Operator runbook\n\n- How to add a new worker machine safely (fresh clone + verify divergence is 0 + start loop)\n- How to recover from a diverged state (the current axon situation): reconcile manually, reset secondaries to origin, restart loops\n- How to migrate existing diverged projects to the new safety model (one-time cleanup)\n- How to stop all execute-loops globally (e.g., during a freeze window)\n\n### Troubleshooting\n\n- 'divergence_detected' event: what caused it and how to recover\n- 'push_rejected' event: what caused it and how to recover\n- 'checkpoint before merge' commits in history: legacy pattern, should not appear after ddx-6aa50e57 lands\n- Multiple 'Merge made by ort' commits: same as above, legacy pattern\n\n### ADR reference\n\nOnce the tracker concurrency bead (ddx-2791dc4f) picks option A / B / C, cite the ADR here so operators understand the coordination model.\n\n## Why this matters\n\nThe P0 beads will prevent future divergence, but operators need to understand WHY the rules exist to avoid bypassing them with --force or manual git operations. Documentation is what keeps the fixes effective a year from now.","id":"ddx-b0374901","issue_type":"task","labels":["docs","execute-loop","orchestration"],"parent":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-ee39eb4","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: document multi-machine topology and operator contract","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:45:17.048232166Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:615:{"acceptance":"- [ ] All six child beads are filed and actionable\n- [ ] The first three (fetch, push, rebase) ship as a coordinated release since none of them are useful without the others\n- [ ] A test harness exists that simulates two parallel workers on the same machine and asserts no merge commits are produced\n- [ ] A test harness exists that simulates two machines with divergent histories and asserts the second machine's claim is refused until reconciliation\n- [ ] docs/EXECUTE-LOOP.md (or equivalent) documents the fetch/rebase/push invariant and the multi-machine topology\n- [ ] The epic closes when all children close AND an end-to-end test has run the loop for 10 iterations across two simulated machines without divergence","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:18:37.269117551Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-bd42f640","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-83ec1430","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-6aa50e57","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2791dc4f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-42dd982c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","depends_on_id":"ddx-b0374901","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-14T12:21:51Z"}],"description":"Root cause epic for the multi-machine divergence problem discovered on axon 2026-04-14 during a planning session. Execute-loop treats master as a private branch and produces divergent histories that never reconcile with origin.\n\n## Evidence\n\nOn axon, two machines running execute-loop (`eitri` and `sindri`) produced 14 days of independent history from a shared merge-base at 27c54ded (2026-04-06):\n\n- eitri: 134 local commits that never reached origin\n- sindri: 336 commits that pushed to origin\n- Same FEAT-026 slot used for two unrelated features (sync protocol on eitri, markdown templates on sindri)\n- Same beads.jsonl touched by both machines with divergent bead IDs and lifecycle state\n- `refs/ddx/iterations/` preservation never fired because merges kept succeeding textually while producing semantically incoherent state\n- 12+ \"Merge made by 'ort' strategy\" commits on 2026-04-13 afternoon on eitri alone, each bringing in a sibling local execute-bead worker's output\n\n## Root causes identified\n\n1. No `git fetch origin` at the top of a worker loop iteration — each bead starts from the worker's stale local HEAD (child bead DDX-agent-fetch)\n2. No `git push` on successful landing — successful work stays trapped on the local branch (child bead DDX-agent-push)\n3. Intra-machine sibling workers land via `git merge --no-edit` (ort strategy) instead of rebase, producing a fan of merge commits instead of a linear history (child bead DDX-agent-rebase)\n4. .ddx/beads.jsonl is a git-tracked mutable ledger with no concurrent-write semantics (child bead DDX-agent-tracker)\n5. No divergence monitoring — the 134/336 split grew silently over two weeks (child bead DDX-agent-monitor)\n6. Multi-machine topology undocumented — which machine is authoritative, who pulls, who pushes (child bead DDX-agent-topology)\n\n## Primary source evidence\n\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_orchestrator.go:59-69 — Merge function uses `git merge --no-edit` with 'ort' strategy, no rebase alternative, commits a 'chore: checkpoint before merge' before attempting each merge\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_commit.go — has ZERO references to push/fetch/origin\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:~320 — worker loop runs Executor.Execute without any fetch-origin or ancestry-check step\n\n## Impact\n\nAny project that has run the execute-loop for more than a few days across more than one machine is silently divergent from origin. Content written by this session cannot be pushed to the axon origin without a multi-hour manual reconciliation. The axon repo currently has two weeks of unshared work on two machines.\n\n## Scope of this epic\n\nSix child beads covering: fetch-before-work, push-on-success, rebase-vs-merge, tracker concurrency, divergence monitoring, multi-machine topology docs. Each is actionable and self-contained.\n\n## Blocks\n\nAny further `ddx agent execute-loop` runs on projects with more than one participating machine should be paused until at least the first three child beads (fetch, push, rebase) are landed. Running the loop in the current state compounds divergence.","id":"ddx-b7ee0d6f","issue_type":"epic","labels":["bug","execute-loop","orchestration","epic"],"notes":"## Reframed 2026-04-14 — design anchor: ddx-8746d8a6\n\nThe six original child beads (bd42f640, 6aa50e57, 83ec1430, 2791dc4f, 42dd982c, b0374901) identified pieces of the gap correctly but did not compose into a coherent end-to-end design. Per an opus-led architectural review on 2026-04-14, the root problem is not that execute-loop's git operations are missing pieces; it is that the whole model is wrong.\n\nTarget model (from the ddx user): human-PR queue. Workers are contributors, a centralized land coordinator is the maintainer, target-ref writes are serialized through a single writer that always sees the current tip.\n\nThe design for this model is in **ddx-8746d8a6 \"execute-loop: adopt human-PR landing model end-to-end (meta redesign)\"**. Treat ddx-8746d8a6 as the authoritative design anchor for this epic. The per-bug children below continue to describe individual gap points, but their FIX is the coordinator pattern, not piecemeal patches:\n\n- ddx-bd42f640 (fetch+ancestry before claim) → subsumed by the coordinator's fetch step\n- ddx-6aa50e57 (rebase instead of merge) → implemented by the coordinator's rebase step (add land-lock note to acceptance)\n- ddx-83ec1430 (push on landing) → part of the coordinator's sequence\n- ddx-42dd982c (divergence monitoring) → reframed as coordinator rejection/conflict metrics\n- ddx-b0374901 (multi-machine docs) → reframed as per-machine coordinator + push-to-origin\n- ddx-2791dc4f (tracker concurrency) → STAYS SEPARATE — tracker is a second shared resource with its own lock story\n\nNew P0 child filed alongside: **ddx-e14efc58 \"server runWorker never calls LandBeadResult; commits silently lost\"** — acute correctness bug discovered during the review. The server path today drops worker commits entirely. Interim fix mirrors the --local closure's land sequence; long-term fix is absorbed by ddx-8746d8a6.\n\nRollout: implement ddx-8746d8a6 first (the coordinator), then close the subsumed children as their work is done, then close this epic. Do NOT try to land the individual children as standalone patches — the piecemeal approach is what got us here.\n\nDo NOT run execute-loop against the ddx repo (or any other repo) under this epic's pattern until the coordinator is in place. Record from the user: \"we may not be separating concerns appropriately — worker should be 'I've been handed a worktree and a task, I will execute that task and make commits, produce progress output, notify done.'\"","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-d16c6e3","status":"closed","title":"Epic: execute-loop git orchestration bugs cause multi-machine divergence","updated_at":"2026-04-14T15:56:16.842137559Z"}
