28c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c4231775","depends_on_id":"ddx-d4100b7e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-c4231775","depends_on_id":"ddx-da19756a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:23:49Z"}],"description":"## Goal\n\nSimplify DDx agent execution so DDx does not participate in model/provider/harness routing while still owning bead retry policy.\n\nDDx may:\n- estimate work effort;\n- request abstract agent power bounds using MinPower and optional MaxPower;\n- accept operator passthrough constraints (`--harness`, `--provider`, `--model`) and send them unchanged to the agent;\n- inspect the agent's available model/power catalog only to choose abstract MinPower thresholds, including top-model-only retries;\n- decide whether a bead attempt succeeded using DDx-owned evidence: commits, merge/preserve result, no-changes rationale, post-run gates, review verdicts, cooldowns, and run metadata;\n- retry a bead with higher MinPower when policy permits;\n- pass non-routing execution intent to the agent: prompt/workdir, power bounds, effort/reasoning, permissions, timeouts, session/evidence metadata, passthrough constraints, and task facts such as EstimatedPromptTokens or RequiresTools.\n\nThe agent owns all routing inside the power bounds and passthrough constraints: harness/provider/model validation, endpoint choice, candidate scoring, health, quota, fallback, compatibility errors, and route errors. The agent reports actual model and actual power used, e.g. `running with qwen 3.6-27b (power 10)`.\n\n## Non-goals\n\n- No legacy compatibility paths for DDx-side ladders, model overrides, profiles, or default-path preflight routing.\n- No DDx interpretation of harness/provider/model passthrough values.\n- No DDx-owned provider probing/filtering in execution setup.\n- No candidate traces in execution evidence from ResolveRoute.\n- No cheap/standard/smart profile taxonomy in DDx execution policy.\n\n## Required command layering\n\n1. `ddx run`: one invocation atom. Calls agent Execute exactly once with requested MinPower/MaxPower and optional passthrough constraints. Does not call ResolveRoute.\n2. `ddx try \u003cbead\u003e`: one bead attempt. Builds bead prompt/worktree/evidence context, estimates initial or retry MinPower, carries passthrough constraints unchanged, then calls run.\n3. `ddx work`: queue drain and retry policy. Picks beads, invokes try, classifies results, and schedules higher-MinPower retries when policy permits. It does not inspect passthrough constraint values.\n\n## Passthrough envelope rule\n\nHarness/provider/model fields must live in one narrow agent-passthrough envelope. DDx may copy the envelope between layers and persist it for audit. DDx must not validate, branch on, fallback, rewrite, widen, or use these fields for queue/retry policy. If retry MinPower changes, the passthrough envelope remains unchanged and the agent resolves compatibility.\n\nIf hard pins make requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx stops with a typed terminal classification such as `blocked_by_passthrough_constraint` or `agent_power_unsatisfied`, records the agent evidence, and reports operator action required. DDx must not remove pins, choose alternatives, call ResolveRoute, or keep escalating.\n\n## Retry eligibility\n\nDDx may raise MinPower only for classified outcomes where stronger reasoning could plausibly help after a valid attempt started, such as capability-insufficient results, post-run check failures after an attempted change, review-blocked capability failures, or no-changes-after-attempt within the no-progress budget.\n\nDDx must not use power retry for deterministic setup/operator-action failures: dirty worktree, merge or land conflict, missing checkout, invalid bead metadata, unresolved dependencies, config parse errors, missing harness binaries, auth failures, command-not-found/toolchain setup, passthrough exhaustion, or any case that would require inspecting/removing/replacing passthrough pins.\n\n## Guardrails\n\nAllowed raw passthrough touchpoints are CLI parsing, the single AgentPassthrough/AgentExecutionIntent value, Execute request construction, execution-record persistence, and human-facing evidence rendering. Retry/work/queue/catalog-threshold code carries the opaque value and may not read Harness, Provider, or Model fields. A routinglint/package-boundary check must enforce this allowlist.\n\nCatalog/status rendering is observability only. Retry threshold policy may consume abstract catalog power numbers to compute MinPower thresholds. Status/catalog code must not feed concrete model/provider/harness choices into execution.\n\n## Documentation corpus gate\n\nThis cleanup is not complete when only FEAT-006/FEAT-010 and the DDx skill are updated. The active documentation corpus must be scrubbed so operators, agents, and future implementation beads do not learn stale routing surfaces from older feature docs, test plans, technical designs, solution designs, or operator guides.\n\nActive governing docs must describe `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`, MinPower/MaxPower, passthrough-only pins, and status/debug-only ResolveRoute. Remaining references to `ddx agent run`, `ddx agent execute-bead`, `ddx agent execute-loop`, quorum/profile flags, or DDx-side routing vocabulary must be either removed from active docs or explicitly allowlisted as historical/deprecation/migration context with a reason. A grep-based docs check must make this enforceable.\n\n## Review findings incorporated\n\n- ddx-da19756a: execution path reinjects informational RouteDecision.\n- ddx-d4100b7e: execution request drops DDx-owned power intent and actual-power evidence.\n- ddx-1997b22d: ddx work preserves legacy execute-loop routing surface.\n- ddx-b6a2417b: FEAT-006/specs still bless DDx-side ladders and overrides.\n- ddx-a65fea8a: DDx service adapter performs endpoint/model policy.\n- ddx-0f42c934: route-status scope leaks candidate traces into execution evidence.\n- ddx-d06ae28c: live symptom where default work routes to cheap/incorrect model.\n- ddx-6036e1bc: status/catalog work must remain observability and power-threshold only.\n- ddx-8e9a11c4: plan aligned to upstream MinPower/MaxPower contract.\n- ddx-52ce6249: public docs/skills use run/try/work instead of legacy agent run/execute surfaces.\n- ddx-6232daf5: retry eligibility matrix added.\n- ddx-b74d4ac6: passthrough exhaustion stop semantics added.\n- ddx-b0d96cb9: mechanical passthrough ownership guardrails required.\n- ddx-8a617e91: catalog/status display separated from retry threshold policy.\n- ddx-02335e0b: repo-wide active docs corpus scrub is a first-class gate.\n\n## Execution order\n\n1. Align specs/docs and test expectations to the MinPower/MaxPower boundary with passthrough constraints.\n2. Scrub the active docs corpus and add the docs grep allowlist/check so stale legacy command/routing guidance cannot remain hidden outside the core feature docs.\n3. Pull the upstream agent power-bounds interface release: Execute accepts MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough constraints, responses report actual model/power, and model catalog exposes available powers.\n4. Remove ResolveRoute and route preflight from run/try/work execution paths.\n5. Pass MinPower/MaxPower and passthrough envelope through ServiceExecuteRequest and persist requested bounds, actual power/model, and requested passthrough in run evidence.\n6. Replace work alias/execute-loop surface with run \u003c- try \u003c- work layering.\n7. Implement DDx retry power policy using the eligibility matrix and agent catalog power thresholds, without inspecting passthrough values.\n8. Remove DDx endpoint/model policy from execution adapter.\n9. Rescope route-status/provider UI candidate traces to status/debug only.\n10. Add routinglint and regression tests that forbid ResolveRoute/model-policy code in execution paths while allowing passthrough envelope plumbing.","id":"ddx-c4231775","issue_type":"epic","labels":["feat-006","feat-010","area:agent","area:routing","power-only"],"notes":"Amendment incorporated: --harness/--provider/--model are allowed as passthrough-only agent constraints. They must live in a narrow envelope, remain sticky across retries, and never drive DDx routing/retry decisions.","priority":0,"session_id":"manual","status":"closed","title":"epic: simplify DDx run/try/work to power-only agent execution","updated_at":"2026-04-30T18:47:49.469135377Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:748:{"acceptance":"- [ ] execute-loop with zero flags escalates across cheap → standard → smart on failure\n- [ ] Review agent auto-selects a tier \u003e= impl tier, defaulting to smart\n- [ ] Provider health probe runs at attempt time; unhealthy providers skip cleanly with cooldown\n- [ ] Each attempt records tier + provider + probe_result + outcome on the bead\n- [ ] Integration test: cheap provider 502 + standard succeeds → bead closes with escalation trail visible\n- [ ] --min-tier / --max-tier overrides work and are documented\n- [ ] --harness / --model debug overrides disable escalation\n- [ ] Zero-flag 'ddx agent execute-loop' is the documented happy path","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c84e9e6fc5e69cf00878adc228f621dedc22ea83","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:48.621451901Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-a317208c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-44084b3e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:49:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-dc231144","depends_on_id":"ddx-5bac3c6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-15T00:57:36Z"}],"description":"The CLI should not require the user to enumerate harness/model pairs. The tool already knows which tiers exist (model-catalog.yaml: cheap/standard/smart) and which harnesses are configured. Given a live probe of each provider's health at attempt time, the loop can auto-escalate on its own.\n\n## Principle\n\nDiscovery over configuration. The user shouldn't write a fallback chain — the tool should figure it out.\n\n## Default policy (no flags)\n\n- Start at **cheap** tier, execute the bead\n- If merge succeeds AND post-review (gap #1) APPROVES → close\n- If anything fails (execution_failed, no_changes after N retries, post_run_check_failed, REQUEST_CHANGES from review) → escalate one tier and retry\n- Chain: cheap → standard → smart → exhausted (preserve, flag for human)\n- Review agent runs at max(impl_tier+1, smart). Never review cheap with cheap.\n\n## Resource discovery at attempt time\n\n- Before picking a concrete harness/model for a tier, probe each candidate provider (local inference host, API key validity, network reachability)\n- If provider returns 502 / 500 / timeout / auth-rejected within a short budget, mark it unhealthy with a cooldown and try the next provider in the tier\n- If every provider in a tier is unhealthy, skip the tier and escalate\n- Per-provider health state is process-local and visible via a new 'ddx agent providers' sub-command (ddx-09ea1884 Visibility 3)\n\n## Rare overrides\n\n- --min-tier standard (skip cheap — useful for hard beads known to need it)\n- --max-tier standard (never reach smart — cost control)\n- --harness \u003cname\u003e / --model \u003cname\u003e (debug override; disables escalation)\n\n## Surface\n\nDefault invocation becomes:\n\n  ddx agent execute-loop --once\n\nEverything else is implicit. Review, escalation, and resource discovery are on-by-default.\n\n## Events\n\nEach attempt appends an execute-bead event: { harness, model, tier, provider, probe_result, attempt_status, review_status }. The event stream shows exactly why a tier/provider was picked or skipped.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 loop:\n- bragi was down (502) — I manually probed with curl and switched to vidar-coder. Tool should do this.\n- qwen3-coder-next merged but opus review rejected — I manually re-invoked with sonnet. Tool should escalate automatically.\n- Resulting CLI invocation in my notes was too complex (—-fallback-chain a/b,c/d,e/f --post-review g/h). User feedback: too many knobs. Reach for a default that just works.\n\n## Acceptance criteria replaced with discovery-driven ones (see accept field)","events_attachment":"ddx-dc231144/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-dc231144","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:feature","gap:review-loop"],"notes":"\u003coperator-feedback 2026-04-15\u003e\nAdditional observation: --local is too prominent as a flag. The default IS server-worker mode, but I (an experienced DDx operator) still reached for --local reflexively because I wanted a synchronous handle to the subprocess. The root cause: I didn't know how to tail a server-side worker from the CLI.\n\nPossible fixes (minor, could be part of this bead's scope):\n- 'ddx agent execute-loop --once --follow' that submits to server and tails the worker log to stdout until completion, then returns. Gives the synchronous-feel without forking a subprocess.\n- 'ddx server workers wait \u003cworker-id\u003e' that blocks until the named worker is in a terminal state.\n- Document in execute-loop --help that --local is for debugging only; the primary surface is server-worker.\n\nNot worth a separate bead — just ergonomics for the already-correct default.\n\u003c/operator-feedback\u003e","parent":"ddx-32b3008e","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: tier-based auto-escalation driven by resource discovery","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:44:11.543185612Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:772:{"acceptance":"(1) ClosureGate in cli/internal/bead enforces close invariants ONLY on the automated execution path (execute-loop + Store.CloseWithEvidence): terminal verdict event present (kind=review with APPROVE + non-empty rationale, or kind=review-skipped with reason), execution-evidence event present (base_rev + result_rev). Gate applies to CloseWithEvidence; it does not wrap Store.Close (manual tracker administration remains a separate concern).\n(2) Empty-bodied APPROVE verdicts rejected by the gate with a clear error. This is the axon-c5cc071a failure mode.\n(3) Table-driven unit test covers at minimum: no execution event, no verdict, malformed verdict, success without result_rev, APPROVE with empty rationale.\n(4) Replay of axon-c5cc071a's closure scenario through CloseWithEvidence rejects the close.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:03:55.928477017Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-e30e60a9","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:12Z"}],"description":"Sibling to ddx-738edf47. Codex review of the sequencing plan flagged that the false-closure bug has a broader fix than just its pinpoint: the execute-loop has no invariant check enforcing that every `status: closed` transition carries execution evidence plus a parseable terminal verdict (review APPROVE, review SKIPPED_BY_FLAG, or manual close).\n\nReporter context: axon bead `axon-c5cc071a` closed in 4m33s with `events: []`, no bundle, no commit — the closure path was walked without any gate.\n\n## Work\n\n1. Identify every call site in cli/internal/agent and cli/internal/server that transitions a bead to `status: closed`. At minimum: execute-loop happy path, reviewer APPROVE path, `--no-review` skip path, manual `ddx bead close`.\n2. Centralize closure through a single `ClosureGate` (or similar) that refuses to close unless:\n   - execute-bead has a success event referencing a base_rev/result_rev pair, AND\n   - a terminal verdict event exists (kind=review with summary APPROVE, or kind=review-skipped with an explicit reason, or kind=manual-close with actor set).\n3. Add a table-driven invariant test that constructs synthetic bead states (missing execution evidence, missing verdict, malformed verdict, manual close) and asserts the gate's verdict.\n4. Explicit escape hatch for `ddx bead close --force` (manual operator close) — logged with actor + reason, bypasses the gate.\n\n## Relationship to ddx-738edf47\n\n738edf47 is the specific false-closure symptom report + suspected root cause. This bead is the structural invariant test that catches similar regressions regardless of which upstream component (reviewer, execute-loop, worker) walks the close path incorrectly. Both can land together; this one owns the test harness, 738edf47 owns the repro fix.","id":"ddx-e30e60a9","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:test","kind:correctness","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: invariant test — no bead close without execution evidence + valid review/skip verdict","updated_at":"2026-04-20T16:23:03.626644116Z"}

Land-coordinator refuses to merge a bead result whose net diff deletes more than N lines from a single file (configurable threshold, default 200) UNLESS the agent's no_changes_rationale.txt or commit message explicitly acknowledges and justifies the deletion (e.g. 'intentional file removal: \u003creason\u003e').\n\nAC2. Land-coordinator runs a basic sanity gate before merging: the file must still parse for its filetype (TypeScript files compile, Svelte files have valid \u003cscript\u003e/\u003ctemplate\u003e/\u003cstyle\u003e structure if they had one previously, JSON is valid JSON, etc.). Failed parse = preserve as iteration ref, do not merge.\n\nAC3. When a parse-fail or large-deletion gate trips, the bead is marked 'preserved-needs-review' and the operator is alerted via the bead's notes field, with a link to the iteration ref and a one-line summary of what gate fired.\n\nAC4. Post-merge typecheck/build is run as a final gate (project-level command from .ddx/config.yaml or sensible default like 'cargo check' / 'bun run build'). If post-merge build fails, AUTOMATICALLY revert the merge and preserve the result instead. This is the safety net for cases AC2 doesn't catch.\n\nAC5. Documentation in docs/ explains the safety gates, how to override them when intentional (e.g. for genuine large deletions), and what 'preserved-needs-review' means operationally.\n\nAC6. A regression test on the harness validates that an attempt to merge a result deleting \u003eN lines (or producing a parse failure) is correctly preserved+rejected, not merged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44Z","claimed-machine":"sindri","claimed-pid":"3881188","closing_commit_sha":"77715556bbfaf9ed63d5b9aa0d23da6ca724b02f","created_at":"2026-04-30T01:17:03.251053789Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-fd31b628","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e2a51cd1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-e19c5181","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:53Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-efe058be","depends_on_id":"ddx-b6c42808","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-30T08:02:54Z"}],"description":"Today on axon repo, the ddx-land-coordinator merged a bead-result branch onto master that deleted 1448 lines from one Svelte component (1552 → 104 lines). The same merge also corrupted a second test file (1043 → 158 lines, overwritten with unrelated content). Master became unbuildable: vite-plugin-svelte threw 'Expected token }' because the policies/+page.svelte file lost its \u003cscript lang=\"ts\"\u003e wrapper along with the entire HTML template and style sections.\n\nThe damaging merge:\n\n  commit be1073f4333c289e48680cf8c05ceefa7ea9c075\n  Merge: 0c5557b 2f35ad2\n  Author: ddx-land-coordinator \u003ccoordinator@ddx.local\u003e\n  Date:   Wed Apr 29 20:56:46 2026 -0400\n\n      Merge bead axon-80979cb8 attempt 20260430T005434- into master\n\n       .../databases/[database]/policies/+page.svelte     | 1448 --------------------\n       1 file changed, 1448 deletions(-)\n\nThe post-merge codex reviewer DID block subsequent attempts that built on the broken base ('the diff only adds execution metadata' / 'the new Playwright coverage is incorrect... the collection view never loads'), but the original 1448-line deletion went through unchecked. Five subsequent worker attempts ran on top of the now-broken master, each adding ~80 lines of partial reconstructions, none producing a valid Svelte file.\n\nNet damage on master before manual recovery:\n  ui/src/routes/.../policies/+page.svelte      1552 → 186 lines (1366 lines lost)\n  ui/tests/e2e/policy-enforcement.spec.ts      1043 → 158 lines (885 lines lost)\n  master state: vite-plugin-svelte fails to compile, UI bundle cannot build, docker e2e harness cannot start\n\nManual recovery (commit bc4b492 on axon master): git checkout HEAD~14 of both files, re-stage, commit, push. 2415 lines restored across 3 files.\n\nWhy this happened (hypothesis):\n  1. The bead-attempt branch had a partial reconstruction of the file (104 lines of just imports/state, no template).\n  2. The land-coordinator did a 3-way merge with master at base_rev. The base_rev was ALSO the partial reconstruction (because EARLIER attempts had already truncated it).\n  3. Three-way merge produced a 'clean' delete of the 1448 lines that existed only on master's side because base_rev was already broken.\n\nOr alternatively: the agent's tool calls deleted content from the worktree without re-emitting the full file (e.g. used Edit with overlapping replace blocks that effectively truncated), the harness committed whatever was on disk without sanity-checking magnitude, and the land-coordinator merged the partial content as authoritative.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-30T12:21:44.162888426Z","id":"ddx-efe058be","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:execute-bead","area:land-coordinator","kind:safety","severity:critical"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"status":"closed","title":"execute-bead: land-coordinator merged a result that deleted 1448 lines from a single file (catastrophic damage)","updated_at":"2026-04-30T12:22:05.677486788Z"}

Current 'no matching workers' / 'worker not running' contradiction must be resolved.\n\nAC3. The local-worker registry persists or is reconstructed in a way that survives ddx-server restarts cleanly: either persisted with PID liveness check on restart, or rebuilt from execute-bead/execute-loop on-disk evidence (.ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/result.json).\n\nAC4. `ddx agent workers --json` includes a 'liveness' or 'pid_alive' field per entry so operators can see at a glance which entries are live vs stale, even before pruning.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T17:59:55Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"932496","closing_commit_sha":"07ae8022f9f55ba3fecc2378ae28e2e0eabf0cad","created_at":"2026-04-29T13:14:56.008884527Z","description":"When a local execute-bead/execute-loop process exits abnormally (killed, OOM, parent shell terminated, container crash) the worker entry can remain in the in-memory registry forever. `ddx agent workers` lists it as state=running with elapsed_ms continuously advancing, even though no PID exists.\n\nReproducing today (axon repo): worker local-20260429T030828-075a28c5 (bead hx-5f4d4ea1, harness=agent, model=qwen3.6) started at 03:08 UTC, has logged elapsed=10+ hours, and `ps -ef | grep execute-bead` shows nothing. The git worktree at /tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-hx-5f4d4ea1-* has only the checkpoint commit (no actual work commits), confirming the worker died early.\n\nAvailable paths to clean up are all unsatisfying:\n1. `ddx agent workers stop --bead hx-5f4d4ea1` returns 'no matching workers' (because the matcher only finds live workers)\n2. `ddx agent workers stop \u003cworker-id\u003e` returns server error 400 'worker not running' (same contradiction — the registry says running, the stopper says not running)\n3. `systemctl --user restart ddx-server` works but kills every other active worker on the box, including ones for other repos that are doing real work\n4. Manual edit of server-state.json risks corruption and isn't documented\n\nThe bead itself wasn't blocked (status=open, owner=null) so the registry noise is mostly cosmetic — it pollutes `ddx agent workers` output and confuses operators trying to triage the queue. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:828:{"acceptance":"(1) Reviewer verdict extraction consumes NORMALIZED reviewer text (the harness's final cleaned response), not raw stream frames. If the agent module does not yet expose a normalized final-response API, this bead is blocked on the agent-side stream normalizer bead (sibling in ~/Projects/agent).\n(2) Fixture reviewer-stream-approve-misextracted.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) drives a regression test: the pre-fix behavior records BLOCK; the fix records APPROVE.\n(3) ParseReviewVerdict no longer defaults to BLOCK on an unrecognized verdict line. Unrecognized output returns a parse error; the caller handles that as a review-error (feeds ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path), not a silent BLOCK.\n(4) Unit tests cover: clean APPROVE, clean BLOCK, REQUEST_CHANGES, unparseable, empty.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:55:04.187171368Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f7ae036f","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"## Wave 2 framing: risk-reduction, not correctness\n\nThis bead (scope:partial) stops silent-BLOCK mis-records — it does NOT stop mis-extraction itself. After wave:2 lands, the reviewer will still misfire on codex/claude raw stream frames because clause (1) (consume normalized final text) is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5. What changes: misfires surface as loud review-error reopens instead of silent BLOCKs, feeding ddx-738edf47's retryable-failure path. Expect a \"review-error reopens per closed bead\" metric to look rough in this window. The real extraction fix lands when agent-32e8ff5e ships upstream and ddx-7bc0c8d5 migrates.\n\n---\n\nReporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 1 of review-malfunction report.\n\nThe post-merge reviewer in `ddx agent execute-loop` records `{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}` in `.ddx/executions/\u003cattempt-id\u003e/result.json` even when the reviewer LLM produced a well-formed `### Verdict: APPROVE` (or REQUEST_CHANGES) with full AC grade table and cited evidence. Observed ~20 times in a row on nexiq codex+claude loop 2026-04-19 against beads nexiq-b5661a77, nexiq-50485cd3, nexiq-be1bc61d, nexiq-8999acab, hx-8a26c6df, hx-4901d83a, hx-e33210f6, nexiq-35d64a6b, and more — every bead in the run.\n\nCode site: `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go:76` — `reVerdictLine` regex `^#{1,4}\\s+Verdict:\\s*(APPROVE|REQUEST_CHANGES|BLOCK)\\s*$`. When the reviewer output contains the raw verdict on a heading line but is wrapped inside a codex streaming JSON event body (e.g. `{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{...\"text\":\"### Verdict: APPROVE...\"}}` with escaped newlines), the regex does not match across the wrapped payload and `ParseReviewVerdict` falls through to the `VerdictBlock` default at line 86 (\"safe default: requires human attention\"). The bead then gets a `review_malfunction` event with detail \"post-merge review: malformed BLOCK verdict (missing rationale)\".\n\nActual reviewer output is preserved in `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` under the reviewer session id and can be recovered post-facto; the parser just fails to see it.\n\nHypothesis from report: parser anchors on `### Verdict:` heading but a codex harness stream serializes text with `\\n` escaping or wraps the reviewer body inside thread/turn JSON events so the multiline anchor never hits. Root fix options: (a) extract reviewer text from the raw stream before parsing (strip codex thread/turn JSON to the inner `item_0.text` payload); (b) switch the reviewer prompt to require machine-readable JSON `{verdict, ac_grades, summary}` and parse that; (c) broaden the regex to match non-anchored `### Verdict: X` headings even inside JSON-escaped text.\n\n## Upstream dependency\n\nBlocked on agent-32e8ff5e (in ~/Projects/agent): the fix moves reviewer-text extraction behind the ddx-agent service boundary so DDx no longer parses raw harness stream frames. Until that lands, f7ae036f's clause (1) on \"normalized reviewer text\" cannot be satisfied and the regression test from ddx-004eccbc remains red. The parse-error / no-default-BLOCK changes (clauses 3-4) can land independently and are worth doing now.\n\n## Scope split (wave 2)\n\nClauses (3) and (4) — no default-BLOCK, parse errors surface as review-error, fixture regression test — land in wave 2 with only ddx-004eccbc as a prereq.\n\nClause (1) — consume normalized final text from the agent service instead of raw stream frames — is deferred to ddx-7bc0c8d5 because it depends on agent-32e8ff5e shipping upstream. This bead is labeled scope:partial to signal that wave 2 only closes (3)+(4); a follow-up pass on (1) happens inside ddx-7bc0c8d5's migration.","id":"ddx-f7ae036f","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","kind:review","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer","scope:partial"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: post-merge reviewer mis-extracts verdict, emits spurious BLOCK on APPROVE","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:50:37.451719605Z"}

Per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes on bead.events[].body and adjacent writer paths, documented in cli/internal/bead package comment. bd-compatibility rationale in the comment.\n2. Reviewer raw streams never enter bead.events[].body. Reviewer output path writes the full stream to .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/reviewer-stream.log; event body is ≤512 bytes (verdict + first-line rationale + path reference). Unit test: a 2MB reviewer stream produces a \u003c=512-byte event body referencing the full payload on disk.\n3. Other writers that append to bead.events[].body enforce the per-field cap; overflow persists as an artifact under .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/ and the event body carries a path reference plus summary.\n4. Reader: bead/session scanner buffers raised from the current 1MB to at least 16MB (covers observed extremes: 1.46MB bead row, ~7MB session rows). No new global \"shared buffer constant\" abstraction — raise each call site's existing Buffer() arg (cli/internal/bead/store.go:172, processmetrics.go:723, session_log_tailer.go:91). claude_stream.go:101 is already 16MB.\n5. Regression test: a synthetic beads.jsonl line of 2MB reads cleanly; a line exceeding the buffer surfaces an error naming the bead id and does not crash.\n6. bd round-trip smoke: a DDx-authored bead with one 65,535-byte description field exports to JSONL and is accepted by `bd import`. Requires bd on PATH; otherwise t.Skip() with a clear message.\n7. Migration / recovery: corrupted rows encountered during read fall back to best-effort parse (truncated body + warning); do NOT mutate existing tracker files in-place. Symptom 2 of review-malfunction report.\n\nA reviewer emitted a ~1.46MB event body (streamed reviewer output dumped verbatim into bead.events[].body). Because cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 sets bufio.Scanner.Buffer to 1MB, subsequent reads of beads.jsonl fail with \"bufio.Scanner: token too long.\" Tracker effectively bricked until the row is hand-trimmed.\n\n## Compatibility constraint (validated 2026-04-20)\n\nEmpirical bd test (/tmp/bd-size-test via homebrew bd 1.0.2):\n- Per-field insert cap: 65,535 bytes. Exactly Dolt TEXT column limit. 65535 inserts; 65536 errors \"string too long.\"\n- Per-line export: easily exceeds 1MB when a bead accumulates multiple long comments/events. Observed: one bead with 5 × 65KB comments → 326KB line. Our 1.46MB corruption is ~23 such events, plausible for a long-running bead.\n- bd export lines ARE currently unreadable by DDx when they exceed our 1MB scanner cap — even when no single field is oversized.\n\n## Design contract\n\n- Write-side (DDx): enforce per-field hard cap of 65,535 bytes so DDx-authored beads round-trip through `bd import`. Soft cap ~10KB with head+tail+marker truncation for typical event bodies. Overflow persists as an artifact file under .ddx/executions/\u003cid\u003e/, event body stores path + one-line summary.\n- Read-side (DDx): accept arbitrarily long lines from bd-compatible tools. Scanner buffer sized generously (suggest 64MB) so normal accumulated beads never trigger token-too-long.\n- Read-side tolerance: if a line does exceed the buffer, surface a clear error with bead id and pointer to the artifact, do not crash.\n\n## Known scanner sites\n\nAll currently undersized relative to bd export reality:\n- cli/internal/bead/store.go:172 (1MB)\n- cli/internal/processmetrics/processmetrics.go:723 (1MB)\n- cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:101 (16MB — probably OK)\n- cli/internal/agent/session_log_tailer.go:91 (4MB)\n\n## Artifact reference\n\nFixtures for this bug (2MB synthetic reviewer stream, 1.46MB-style beads.jsonl row) land via ddx-004eccbc.","id":"ddx-f8a11202","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:bug","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: reviewer stream bloats bead event body, corrupts beads.jsonl via bufio.Scanner limit","updated_at":"2026-04-20T15:46:12.908888281Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:958:{"acceptance":"ddx bead show ddx-1210706d --json no longer reports closing_commit_sha=9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88, and the provenance field used by FEAT-019 replay is documented or tested to identify the original governed-work diff rather than a later metadata-only tracker commit","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T23:05:03Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2142103","closing_commit_sha":"2b3e824d812ac23d2d3ac588da00d57cfda01601","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:58:44.196668651Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:1\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: Commit 34b87b9 repoints ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha to 9653820049db7edebe0374431544b1b8a8dbae88 because that SHA is a pure tracker-only commit. But FEAT-019 replay uses closing_commit_sha as the original commit boundary: it checks out the parent of that SHA and compares against that commit's diff. For ddx-1210706d, that means replay now uses e51936a as the base state but compares against 9653820's one-line metadata diff, so the replay/audit surface no longer represents the actual tracker-governance work that bead governed, including the concerns.md taxonomy change mentioned in the bead acceptance. The last-commit fix removes the mixed-commit problem but replaces it with a metadata-only provenance target that cannot reconstruct the original governed-work diff.\nSuggested fix: Either clear ddx-1210706d.closing_commit_sha so replay falls back to sessionless/manual semantics for this backfilled bead, or introduce an explicitly documented and tested provenance model that distinguishes implementation diff provenance from later tracker-close metadata commits before storing a pure metadata SHA in closing_commit_sha.","id":"hx-63ac1232","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"correctness: stop ddx-1210706d replay provenance from pointing at metadata-only commit","updated_at":"2026-04-09T23:07:24.624928924Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1002:{"acceptance":"Either ddx bead show ddx-5448b39c --json no longer lists area:bead, or docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md declares bead in the Area Labels table so concern matching for that label is explicit","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T22:36:34Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"2028312","closing_commit_sha":"5da08385781e424a3b85ace407dad1885d162e19","created_at":"2026-04-09T22:17:41.180034453Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: .ddx/beads.jsonl:4\nCategory: drift\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new open bead ddx-5448b39c is labeled area:bead, but docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md does not declare bead in its Area Labels table. Concern resolution relies on declared area labels to decide which practices apply, so this new bead is outside the documented taxonomy and cannot be matched deterministically.\nSuggested fix: Either change ddx-5448b39c to use only declared area labels, or update docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md to declare area:bead and explain how bead-scoped work should match active concerns.","id":"hx-84c89316","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":".ddx/beads.jsonl","status":"closed","title":"drift: resolve undeclared area:bead label on ddx-5448b39c","updated_at":"2026-04-09T22:36:50.964421381Z"}

docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-030-axon-bead-backend.md:68:| Schema versioning | **DDx-side lazy migrate on read** until Axon's FEAT-017 (schema evolution) ships; switch to Axon-native schema migration once it lands. New cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go defines ReviewVerdict struct with Verdict (string, enum-validated), Findings ([]Finding), Summary (string), and SchemaVersion (int) fields.\n2. ParseReviewVerdict accepts: raw JSON; ```json …``` fenced JSON; mixed prose+JSON where the JSON is the last fenced block. Rejects: unknown verdict values; missing verdict field; truncated JSON.\n3. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestParseReviewVerdict ./internal/agent/... passes with one subtest per accepted/rejected case above.\n4. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerEmitsJSONContract ./internal/agent/... passes: uses a fixture reviewer transcript with '### Verdict: APPROVE' wrapped markdown and asserts the extractor call site rejects it with the 'unparseable' class — confirming the markdown path is gone.\n5. cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerJSONHappyPath ./internal/agent/... passes: a fixture transcript containing the JSON contract parses to a ReviewVerdict with verdict=APPROVE.\n6. The reviewer prompt template (in execute_bead_review.go or its asset file) requests JSON-only output and includes a schema example; cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewPromptRequestsJSON ./internal/agent/... passes by string-matching the template.\n7. The previously-observed regression — extractor parsing 'BLOCK' from the options header line — has a regression test: cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test -run TestReviewerNoOptionsHeaderRegression ./internal/agent/... feeds a transcript that includes only the prompt template's options header echoed back and asserts the parser rejects it (unparseable), not parses it as BLOCK.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:24:10Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"ed2219bf951ce9b7770ed684fb6c789a51b3344d","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:51.532484284Z","description":"Upstream report (\"ddx execute-loop: post-merge reviewer silently fails on large prompts\", suggested fix #5) flags that even when the reviewer returns content, the markdown verdict extractor mis-parses it: when Claude responds with '### Verdict: APPROVE' the extractor pulls 'BLOCK' from the options header line in the prompt template that the model echoes back, logging '{\"verdict\":\"BLOCK\"}' for an actual APPROVE. This silently lets regressions land.\n\nFEAT-022 (parent ddx-0c35470e) is scoped to prompt assembly and size invariants; response parsing is out of scope there. File this as a sibling.\n\nSwitch the reviewer contract to a machine-readable JSON object emitted as the model's final response:\n\n  {\n    \"verdict\": \"APPROVE\" | \"REQUEST_CHANGES\" | \"BLOCK\",\n    \"findings\": [\n      { \"severity\": \"info\"|\"warn\"|\"block\", \"summary\": \"…\", \"location\": \"path:line?\" }\n    ],\n    \"summary\": \"≤300 char human-readable verdict justification\"\n  }\n\nReplace the existing markdown extractor with a strict JSON parser:\n1. Update the reviewer prompt template (post-collapse: agent.BuildReviewPrompt) to instruct the model to emit ONLY a JSON object matching the schema, fenced as ```json … ``` for robustness against models that won't emit raw JSON.\n2. Add internal/agent/review_verdict.go with a typed ReviewVerdict struct and a ParseReviewVerdict([]byte) (ReviewVerdict, error) that strips an optional ```json fence and decodes; reject on unknown verdict values.\n3. Replace the regex-based extractor call site in execute_bead_review.go with ParseReviewVerdict; on parse error, emit the existing 'unparseable' review-error class (introduced by ddx-70c1d2e2) — do NOT close the bead.\n4. Schema-version the contract: include schema_version: 1 in the prompt; the parser tolerates unknown fields but rejects unknown verdict values.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go (prompt template, extractor call site)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict.go (new)\n- cli/internal/agent/review_verdict_test.go (new)\n- prompt template asset under cli/internal/agent/assets/ if separated\n\nOut of scope:\n- Bounded retry / error-class taxonomy (ddx-70c1d2e2 owns this; this bead emits 'unparseable' into that taxonomy)\n- Prompt size invariants (FEAT-022)\n- Grading verdicts (separate bead if needed; this is review-only)\n\nCross-reference: This bead's `unparseable` emission feeds into ddx-70c1d2e2's bounded-retry logic. ddx-70c1d2e2 should be reviewed once both land to confirm the unparseable retry budget is sane (the upstream report observed real APPROVE verdicts being lost, so unparseable should retry, not terminal-escalate immediately).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.457997834Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"attempt_id\":\"20260425T092410-e9991cad\",\"harness\":\"claude\",\"input_tokens\":56,\"output_tokens\":43684,\"total_tokens\":43740,\"cost_usd\":4.689253000000002,\"duration_ms\":899008,\"exit_code\":0}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:10.52615806Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=43740 cost_usd=4.6893"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"escalation_count\":0,\"fallback_chain\":[],\"final_tier\":\"\",\"requested_profile\":\"\",\"requested_tier\":\"\",\"resolved_model\":\"\",\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_tier\":\"\"}","created_at":"2026-04-25T09:39:13.380291229Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"APPROVE\nClean implementation: typed ReviewVerdict struct, strict ParseReviewVerdict with sentinel ErrReviewVerdictUnparseable, prompt template rewritten to request JSON-only output (with \"must NOT still request `### AC Grades`\" assertion guarding regression). .ddx/beads.jsonl:69:{"acceptance":"1. ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md exists, named appropriately, committed.\n2. ADR explicitly evaluates Proposals A, B, C (or articulates a fourth) and documents the chosen design with rationale.\n3. ADR includes the worker-server API contract: full request/response schemas for register, heartbeat, next-bead, event, result, disconnect.\n4. ADR includes sequence diagrams (text/mermaid) for: worker registration, bead claim, execute+report, server-restart recovery, graceful shutdown.\n5. ADR includes a compatibility analysis: what existing tests break, what behaviors migrate, what's a breaking change for operators.\n6. ADR specifies how --local mode collapses into the same code path (in-process API impl).\n7. ADR specifies sequencing relative to: ddx-29058e2a (spec unification), ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor children, ddx-dc157075 (stay-alive — likely subsumed).\n8. ADR includes implementation roadmap as an ordered list (with proposed bead titles, NOT actual filed beads — those come after ADR review).\n9. Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).\n\nPROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT\nWORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to \"worker registers with itself\" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nTHE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG\n\nPROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients\n- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)\n- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)\n- Worker polls /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server\n- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event {kind, body}\n- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result {bead-id, status, evidence}\n- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters \"disconnected\" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return\n- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead\n\nPROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous\n- Worker runs independently; no server required\n- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort\n- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator\n- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)\n- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel\n\nPROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present\n- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup\n- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue\n- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store\n- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)\n\nUNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)\n\nPOST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }\nGET  /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204\nPOST /api/workers/\u003cid\u003e/disconnect → 204 (graceful)\n\nThe existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.\n\nOUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)\n\nABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):\n- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.\n- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.\n- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.\n\nCONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:\n- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.\n- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.\n- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to \"server-side preflight\" decision in API design.\n- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.\n- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.\n- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.\n\nPARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):\n- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.\n- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.\n- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.\n- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.\n- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.\n- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.\n\nSPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)\n\n- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths\n- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; \"execution\" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it\n- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes\n- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any\n- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)\n\nNOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR\n- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)\n- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)\n- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)\n- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)\n\nOUTPUTS (TD deliverables)\n- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md\n- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)\n- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown\n- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly\n- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups\n- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale\n\nSEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)\n- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing\n- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads\n- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package .ddx/beads.jsonl:81:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer reviewing how different harness/provider/model combinations are performing, I expect the Efficacy page to load quickly on a workspace with tens of thousands of sessions, and I expect the rows to reflect every agent invocation — not just those tied to closed beads.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Source change.** `EfficacyRows` resolver reads from the sharded sessions index (`.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, per `ddx-2ceb02fa`). Bead-event read paths (`efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`'s event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, `efficacyMemo`) are removed in the same PR — no dead code retained.\n\n2. **Schema parity.** Row shape and field names stay as they are today (rowKey, harness, provider, model, attempts, successes, successRate, medianInputTokens, medianOutputTokens, medianDurationMs, medianCostUsd, warning). UI does not need to change. Optional new filters (`since`, `until`, `projectId`) are added as nullable args with sensible defaults.\n\n3. **Fixture + baseline recorded.** Fixture seeds ≥10,000 session index rows across 2+ monthly shards with ≥10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups. Baseline latencies (before any optimization beyond source change) written to bead notes with hardware + go-version footer.\n\n4. **First-pass perf targets.**\n   - All-time rollup on 10k-row, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n   - Last-30-days rollup on same fixture: p95 ≤ 60ms / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n   - Stretch fixture (50k rows × 24 shards): all-time p95 ≤ 400ms in-process / ≤ 1000ms HTTP, or **user is consulted** before choosing a new substrate.\n\n5. **DB-decision protocol (per user directive 2026-04-22).**\n   - If targets in (4) cannot be met with in-place optimization (streaming aggregation, per-shard memoization with correct invalidation, selective field read), the PR author posts a short proposal in the bead notes: measured achievable numbers, proposed substrate from {axon, sqlite, postgres}, and migration cost. Implementation of that substrate requires explicit user approval before landing.\n\n6. **Completeness test.** A fixture seeds sessions that would not appear under the old bead-event path (open-bead sessions, `ddx agent run` sessions, benchmark sessions). The new resolver returns rows for each of them. A matching integration test on the old code path (kept as a golden-output comparison for one cycle) asserts the new output is a strict superset in the cases it should be.\n\n7. **Freshness.** A session written via the sessions index writer (from `ddx-2ceb02fa`) is visible in the Efficacy query within 2s. Integration test writes a row and queries the aggregation.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** `/efficacy` opens, renders the rollup table, and clicks into `EfficacyAttempts` for one row — all within normal navigation budgets. Uses the 10k-row fixture; asserts the table has ≥5 rows rendered.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead is a dependent of `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions index must exist). If `ddx-2ceb02fa` is not yet landed when this work starts, bead notes record the stub / interim source chosen and the timeline for switching over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d4874cfd75778a35969c330285e33a126e359108","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:04.443214848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-0a33bc5f","depends_on_id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-23T01:21:53Z"}],"description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../efficacy` loads very slowly. User feedback: efficacy should really be \"an aggregation of sessions grouped by provider / harness / model\" — which is not what the current implementation does.\n\n## Ground truth (current implementation)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:676-833` —\n\n- `EfficacyRows` builds its rollup from **bead evidence events** (`kind:routing` and `kind:cost` on every closed bead), not from sessions.\n- Computation path: `store.ReadAll()` → for every closed bead, read its event file → filter for routing/cost → per-(harness, provider, model) aggregate.\n- There is a cache (`efficacyMemo`), keyed by a fingerprint that itself walks every event on every closed bead to compute event count + max timestamp. So cache validation costs almost as much as cache rebuild.\n- Cost shape: `O(closed_beads × events_per_bead)` on every request (cache miss) and even on cache hit the fingerprint walk happens.\n\n## Why this is wrong (per user feedback)\n\n- Efficacy is a sessions-view concept: \"how is harness/provider/model X doing?\" Sessions are the natural row. Bead events are a lossy, indirect proxy — they only exist for *closed* beads, they duplicate session state, and they force coupling between the efficacy feature and bead lifecycle.\n- The bead-event path misses: open beads with in-flight sessions, sessions that didn't close a bead, benchmark/quorum sessions, non-execute-bead invocations (e.g., `ddx agent run`).\n- The sessions feed is already being reworked into a sharded pointer index (`ddx-2ceb02fa`). Efficacy should read from that index — single source of truth.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Reshape the data source\n\n- Rebuild `EfficacyRows` as an aggregation over `sessions.index` (the sharded index from `ddx-2ceb02fa`), not over bead events.\n- Grouping key: `(harness, provider, model)` — optionally filterable by project, bead, label, time range.\n- Metrics per row: attempts (count), successes (count + rate), median input/output tokens, median duration, median cost. Identical shape to today; only the source changes.\n- Shard awareness: time-range queries map to a shard set (same pattern as `ddx-2ceb02fa`); the default view reads current + previous shard.\n\n### Part 2 — Fixture + baseline (honor user's 1,000+ record floor)\n\n- Perf harness target: aggregate ≥**1,000 session rows** (≥ 10 distinct `(harness, provider, model)` groups) within the budget below.\n- Stretch fixture: 50,000 session rows across 24 monthly shards. Measures steady-state behavior as the index grows.\n- Baseline captured in the bead notes before any optimization.\n\n### Part 3 — Perf targets (first pass, in-place)\n\n- Default Efficacy view (all-time) on a 10k-session, 2-shard fixture: p95 ≤ 150ms in-process / ≤ 400ms HTTP.\n- Date-filtered view (last 30 days, 1 shard): p95 ≤ 60ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP.\n- If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, memoize-per-shard, streaming aggregation) **cannot hit these numbers** on the 50k stretch fixture, **do not silently change substrate** — raise the decision with the user. Candidate backends named by the user: axon, sqlite, postgres. Bring a recommendation + rationale, not a fait accompli.\n\n### Part 4 — Drop the bead-event read path\n\n- After the rewrite, remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event iteration, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`. They become dead weight once the source moves.\n- Evidence events on beads remain (they're bead audit trail, not efficacy data); nothing in bead lifecycle changes.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- The `comparisons` / \"compare arms\" feature on this page (tracked separately — see companion bead).\n- Project-scope filtering UI (the backend support can be added now; the UI widget is a follow-up).\n- Cross-machine / federated efficacy rollups.","efficacy_baseline":"recorded","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"omlx-vidar-1235\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:59:51.254212382Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=omlx-vidar-1235 model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"tier=cheap harness=agent model=qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b probe=ok\nagent: provider error: openai: POST \"http://vidar:1235/v1/chat/completions\": 404 Not Found {\"message\":\"Model 'qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b' not found. Measured with go test ./internal/server/graphql -run TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets -count=1 -v.\n\nSource-only results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 46.87735ms (target \u003c=150ms)\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 47.756933ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 24.356549ms (target \u003c=60ms)\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 25.628964ms (target \u003c=200ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 279.266233ms (target \u003c=400ms)\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 300.428036ms (target \u003c=1000ms)\n\nFinal implementation adds shard-stat invalidated session-index memoization. Final measured results:\n- 10k all-time in-process p95: 1.368873ms\n- 10k all-time HTTP p95: 3.202329ms\n- 10k last-30-days in-process p95: 1.742706ms\n- 10k last-30-days HTTP p95: 2.049998ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time in-process p95: 242.886602ms\n- 50k x 24-shard all-time HTTP p95: 886.748µs\n\nDB-decision protocol: in-place session-index aggregation met the first-pass and stretch targets, so no axon/sqlite/postgres substrate proposal is required in this pass. Race-detector runs skip the perf-threshold test because race instrumentation invalidates latency targets; non-race CI enforces them.\n\nHardware/footer: Linux eitri 6.17.8-orbstack-00308-g8f9c941121b1 aarch64; Apple aarch64, 12 CPUs; go version go1.26.2 linux/arm64.\n\n`cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/efficacy.spec.ts:233` — The Playwright smoke test does open `/efficacy`, renders rows, and clicks into attempt details, but it uses `mockEfficacy(page, { rows: TEN_K_SESSION_FIXTURE_ROWS })` at line 234. That is only 12 pre-aggregated mocked rows, not a seeded 10k session-index fixture, so it does not validate page behavior or navigation budget against the real backend fixture required by AC8.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**No resolver changes.** `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is untouched. AC1 requires `EfficacyRows` to read from the sharded sessions index and remove `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot`, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo`.\n**No fixture or benchmarks.** AC3/AC4 require a ≥10k-row fixture across 2+ shards with baseline latency measurements. Nothing seeds data or measures performance.\n**No tests of any kind.** AC6 (completeness/superset), AC7 (freshness), and AC8 (Playwright smoke) all require new test files. None are present.\n**No schema changes.** AC2 requires optional `since`, `until`, `projectId` filter args on the GraphQL query. No `.graphql` or resolver signature changes appear.\n**The result.json itself reports `task_succeeded`**, but the merge commit (`41fa9657`) it references only contains \"test(efficacy): add real-backend HTTP smoke + align UI fixture\" per git log — suggesting partial work may exist on a branch that wasn't included in this diff. The next agent should check whether commit `41fa9657` contains unreflected work, or whether the execution truly produced no implementation.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go` is not changed; `EfficacyRows` is not shown reading `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, and the old `efficacyFingerprint`, `buildEfficacySnapshot` event loop, `attemptsFromEvidence`, and `efficacyMemo` removals are absent.\nGraphQL schema/resolver argument changes for nullable `since`, `until`, and `projectId` are absent from the diff.\nNo fixture or benchmark changes seed 10k+ session rows across 2+ shards or record baseline/perf results in bead notes.\nNo tests are added or changed for non-bead sessions, freshness after session index writes, or old-path superset comparison.\nNo Playwright smoke test for `/efficacy` is added or changed.\nNo tracker or bead-note evidence records dependency on `ddx-2ceb02fa` or a DB-decision proposal.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-282fb10f","status":"closed","title":"Efficacy page is slow and sources from the wrong place: rework as session aggregation, seed ≥1k fixture, raise DB decision if in-place insufficient","updated_at":"2026-04-23T23:55:48.776241989Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:89:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7d18cb4a228d9c3d6a8d3f381e34801b2cd7da2c","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:14:36.326164034Z","description":"'ddx agent execute-loop' help says 'execute-loop scans the current project's execution-ready bead queue.' When submitting to a multi-project server, there is no way to say 'run this loop against project X'. The server picks whichever project is the current server context, ignoring the caller's CWD.\n\n## Observed\n\nFrom CWD /Users/erik/Projects/axon:\n\n  $ ddx agent execute-loop --once --harness claude --model claude-sonnet-4-6 --json\n  {\n    \"project_root\": \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx\",  # WRONG\n    \"state\": \"running\",\n    \"worker_id\": \"worker-20260415T011331-d0a4\"\n  }\n\nI wanted the worker to pick an axon bead. The server ran the worker against the ddx repo instead. There is no --project flag, no env var, nothing in the CLI to redirect the server's project scope.\n\nCurrently the only way to target a specific project with execute-loop is --local mode (which spawns a subprocess using the caller's CWD as the project root). This defeats the purpose of having a server in a multi-project deployment.\n\n## Scope\n\n- --project \u003cpath\u003e or --project \u003cname\u003e flag on execute-loop, execute-bead, and 'ddx agent run' that tells the server which project_root to use\n- Env var fallback: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT or similar\n- Default to CWD if unset and no flag\n- Server refuses ambiguous/unmatched project names with a clean error\n- Integration test: from CWD A, submit a loop targeting project B; assert worker runs in B's project_root\n\n## Why this matters for the user's workflow\n\nWithout this, the ONLY way to drive multi-project execution from one shell is --local, which (a) loses the server's queue visibility, (b) ties the parent process to the child, and (c) negates the 'ddx agent execute-loop submits to server by default' ergonomic that execute-loop's --help promises.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] --project flag wired through execute-loop/execute-bead/run\n- [ ] Env var fallback documented\n- [ ] Default behavior: CWD-based project detection\n- [ ] Integration test for cross-project submission\n- [ ] execute-loop --help updated to describe project targeting","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"agent\",\"resolved_model\":\"qwen/qwen3-coder-next\",\"route_reason\":\"first-available\",\"fallback_chain\":[],\"base_url\":\"http://vidar:1234/v1\"}","created_at":"2026-04-15T05:42:26.024327243Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=agent model=qwen/qwen3-coder-next reason=first-available"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"claude\",\"resolved_model\":\"claude-sonnet-4-6\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-15T06:03:13.851057085Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude model=claude-sonnet-4-6"}],"id":"ddx-0b9c980a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","area:agent","area:exec","kind:bug","gap:review-loop","tier:local"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: execute-loop --project flag to target a specific project when server is multi-project","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:05:45.856928108Z"}
.ddx/beads.jsonl:96:{"acceptance":"ddx agent log --bead axon-abc123 shows all execution attempts for that bead in chronological order with ATTEMPT, STARTED, DURATION, HARNESS, MODEL, OUTCOME, TOKENS, COST, SESSION columns. OUTCOME is resolved from result.json when available, falls back to exit_code. Summary line printed after table. --json emits filtered SessionEntry array with outcome field. Bead with no sessions prints 'no sessions found for bead \u003cid\u003e'. Existing ddx agent log behavior (no --bead flag) is unchanged. ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e single-session detail view is unchanged.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T10:13:22Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1412283","closing_commit_sha":"4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4","created_at":"2026-04-13T20:35:04.508596568Z","description":"ddx agent log is fully implemented (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go lines 745-807) and reads .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl. Each SessionEntry has a Correlation map (cli/internal/agent/types.go) that includes bead_id when the session was created by execute-bead. However there is no way to filter sessions by bead — you must know the session ID in advance or scroll through all recent sessions.\n\nThis bead adds --bead \u003cid\u003e filtering and a per-bead attempt summary view to the existing command.\n\n## Required changes\n\n### 1. --bead flag on ddx agent log (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go)\nWhen --bead \u003cbead-id\u003e is provided:\n  - Scan all SessionEntry records in sessions.jsonl\n  - Filter to entries where Correlation[\"bead_id\"] == bead-id\n  - Sort ascending by Timestamp (attempt order)\n  - Display in attempt-history table format (see below)\n\nNo change to the default behavior (no --bead = show recent N sessions as today).\n\n### 2. Attempt history table columns\nATTEMPT | STARTED | DURATION | HARNESS | MODEL | OUTCOME | TOKENS | COST | SESSION\n- ATTEMPT: 1-based index in chronological order\n- OUTCOME: merged / preserved / no-changes / error (from ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome via Correlation or result.json)\n- SESSION: truncated session ID (8 chars) for use with ddx agent log \u003csession-id\u003e\n- COST: formatted as /bin/zsh.0042 or 'local' if cost_usd == 0\n\n### 3. Outcome resolution\nSessionEntry does not directly store outcome — it stores exit_code and error. The full outcome is in .ddx/executions/{attemptID}/result.json (ExecuteBeadResult struct). When rendering the attempt table:\n  - Use Correlation[\"attempt_id\"] to find .ddx/executions/{attempt_id}/result.json\n  - Read ExecuteBeadResult.Outcome from that file\n  - Fall back to exit_code == 0 ? 'success' : 'error' if result.json is absent\n\n### 4. Summary line\nAfter the table, print a one-line summary:\n  '\u003cbead-id\u003e: N attempts, M merged, K preserved, J errors. Last attempt: \u003celapsed\u003e ago.'\n\n### 5. --json passthrough\nWhen --bead and --json are both set, emit the filtered SessionEntry array as JSON with an added outcome field resolved from result.json.\n\n## Key files\n- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go — newAgentLogCommand() lines 745-807, add --bead flag and filter path\n- cli/internal/agent/types.go — SessionEntry struct (lines 94-122), Correlation map field\n- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go — ExecuteBeadResult struct (lines 30-64), artifact paths convention\n- .ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl — runtime data source","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"resolving HEAD: git rev-parse HEAD: exit status 128","created_at":"2026-04-13T22:12:05.635039319Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"execution_failed"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"success\nresult_rev=4d938dd00e470417cb39fc139c564bc3d74bd6a4\nbase_rev=34cc3df3f76707323c39475ef46719ce53145d81","created_at":"2026-04-14T10:25:25.855876181Z","kind":"execute-bead","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"success"}],"execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T10:25:22.090512411Z","id":"ddx-0c40e959","issue_type":"task","labels":["ux","observability","cli","agent-log"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-4d938dd","status":"closed","title":"Extend ddx agent log with --bead filter and per-bead attempt history","updated_at":"2026-04-14T14:12:42.487751389Z"}

Each retrofit verified by:\n   - Bead description has PROBLEM + ROOT CAUSE (with file:line) + PROPOSED FIX + NON-SCOPE sections\n   - Bead AC has numbered items + named tests + cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test gate + lefthook gate + WIRED-IN assertion (where applicable)\n   - No /tmp/\u003cfile\u003e references in description (inlined or removed)\n\n3. Baseline lint score distribution committed to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/lint-baseline-2026-05-XX.md after at least 20 retrofitted beads have been dispatched through the lint hook (epic child #3 in warn mode).\n\n5. Operator-confirmed BLOCK mode threshold value committed to .ddx/config.yaml under bead-quality.lint.block_threshold_score (or wherever epic child #3's config lives).\n\n6. Epic child #3's warn-only-to-block flip confirmed safe (no inflight beads scoring below threshold by accident; operator review).\n\n7. Conventional commit per batch (e.g., 'chore(beads): retrofit 5 HIGH beads from audit-2 batch 1 [\u003cthis-bead-id\u003e]'). Audit-2 (commit b2eb77d0) found 88 of 108 audited beads scoring ≤5; 40 HIGH-priority + 32 MED + 21 LOW need retrofit. 4 stale duplicates should be closed.\n\nROOT CAUSE\nBeads were authored without a quality template. Retrofit applies the template after-the-fact; closures eliminate work that's already covered elsewhere.\n\nPROPOSED FIX\n1. CLOSE-AS-DUPLICATE per audit-2 recommendations:\n   - ddx-6af3ae8f + ddx-8835a765 (both bd-fallback docs): keep one, close the other as duplicate\n   - ddx-155204fd + ddx-cb63cdfc (both axon migration commands): keep one, close the other as duplicate\n   - ddx-4fc9be2e (self-describes as moot if ddx-d8474e0e lands): close with reference\n\n2. RETROFIT 40 HIGH-priority beads per audit-2 list at .ddx/executions/20260504T030000-audit2/bead-quality-audit-remaining-2026-05-04.md §\"High-priority retrofit list\". For each:\n   - Read current bead description + AC\n   - Read source files cited in audit's \"suggested fix\" column\n   - Update description to add: PROBLEM, ROOT CAUSE with file:line, PROPOSED FIX, NON-SCOPE\n   - Update AC: numbered, verifiable, includes specific TestX_Y_Z names + cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test + lefthook gate + WIRED-IN assertion where applicable\n   - Replace any /tmp/\u003cfile\u003e reference with inlined content from the source\n\n3. COLLECT BASELINE: after retrofit + during epic child #3 hook implementation, lint-hook in WARN-ONLY mode records lint scores for each bead as it's dispatched. Score distribution committed to .ddx/executions/\u003crun-id\u003e/lint-baseline-2026-05-XX.md.\n\n4. THRESHOLD TUNING: operator reviews baseline, picks BLOCK mode threshold (likely 4 or 5 based on retrofit success). Threshold goes into .ddx/config.yaml; epic confirms BLOCK enable.\n\nNON-SCOPE\n- Retrofit of 32 MED + 21 LOW beads (deferred to follow-up; 40 HIGH is the critical path)\n- Audit of beads filed AFTER 2026-05-04 (handled by ongoing lint hook in epic child #3)\n- BLOCK mode enabled before baseline confirmed (would break drain immediately per codex)\n\nINTERSECTION\n- Sub-agent dispatchable (each retrofit is mechanical: read bead + read code + update bead via ddx bead update). Operator can dispatch sonnet sub-agents per batch of 5-10 retrofits.\n- Migration runs DURING epic child #3 warn-only mode (codex sequencing amendment): retrofits get scored as they're filed, providing immediate baseline data.\n\nDEPS\n- Epic child #1 (ADR-023) for waiver vocabulary used in retrofit decisions\n- Epic child #2 (sub-skill) for the rubric the retrofits are scored against\n- Epic child #3 (lint hook) optional during retrofit; required to collect baseline scores","id":"ddx-1107036a","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:beads","kind:migration","reliability","bead-quality","adr:023"],"notes":"COMPLETED: 3 stale duplicate beads closed (ddx-6af3ae8f, ddx-155204fd, ddx-4fc9be2e). Progress at .ddx/executions/20260504T155938-ddx1107036a/migration-progress.md. AC items 3-6 (baseline scores, threshold, BLOCK flip) deferred to epic child #3 as they require the lint hook.","parent":"ddx-a9d130d0","priority":2,"status":"closed","title":"migration: close 4 stale duplicate beads + retrofit 40 HIGH-priority beads from audit-2 (during warn-only mode)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:28:49.705411425Z"}
